title: Index corruption + catastrophic clobber on corrupt read
description: Concurrent/torn writes corrupted index.yaml; then upsertIndexEntry treated a corrupt read as empty (?? emptyIndex) and rewrote the index with just the new entry, orphaning all others (professor_mari: 1269 entry files vs 33 index rows). Fixes: (1) atomic writeYaml via temp+rename — no torn files, kill-safe; (2) route promotion/cleanup writes through serialized mutateIndex (no more unlocked writeIndex); (3) upsertIndexEntry refuses to overwrite a present-but-unreadable index; (4) scripts/repair-indexes.mjs rebuilds indexes from entry files.
close_reason: Atomic writes + full serialization + upsert clobber-guard + repair script. tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: [feature] Memory tab governs only chat scope — 4761 character memories are ungoverned
description:
User directive 2026-06-19: ungoverned character-scope memory is not acceptable. The memory tab's lanes load ONLY scope=chat (marinara-extender.js loadPanelData -> /api/entries?scope=chat). Everything [remember:] writes goes to CHARACTER scope (durable, cross-chat) and never appears in the lanes — so it can't be sorted, deleted, or restored from the panel. DATA (professor_mari, 2026-06-19): character hot index = 4761 entries; the current chat (c7Ui03XMg6bV7lVXOyz-8) = 426 chat-scope entries. The lanes show/govern the 426; the 4761 are invisible and unmanageable. The Retired view IS character scope, so the panel already crosses scopes inconsistently.

ACCEPTANCE (either is defensible; ungoverned character scope is NOT): the memory tab must let the user see and manage character memories — EITHER (a) show character memories alongside chat ones with a clear visual scope marker per row/group, OR (b) a scope toggle (Chat | Character) on the memory tab. Delete/restore/sort must operate correctly in whichever scope is shown (removeEntry/loadDeleted/sort currently hardcode scope=chat — must take the displayed entry's scope). Relates to 734 (recoverable delete) and 3pl (Retired, already character scope).

NOTE: separate live bug observed same session — deleting a chat-scope entry from the panel does nothing and NO chat has a cold index (no soft-delete has succeeded). Being diagnosed; may be a restart/CSRF/handler issue. Track the scope work here; fix the delete bug before closing since this feature depends on delete working per-scope.
notes: Code-complete + pushed (a05bda3), NOT browser-verified. This chat | Character scope toggle + Newest/Most used/Oldest sort; lanes cap rendered rows (show-all). All client-side — reload tab, no sidecar restart. VERIFY: toggle to Character → ~4761 entries in collapsed lanes w/ counts → expand → 30 shown + 'Show all' → delete/restore a char entry round-trips → sort orders apply in both scopes. Pure UI; once confirmed, close. Follow-ups parked: search/filter (needs focus-restore), batch delete (user deferred).
close_reason: Verified in-browser 2026-06-20: Character scope lists durable memories, large lanes cap with show-all, delete/restore round-trips in character scope, sort (Newest/Most used/Oldest) works in both scopes. Scope toggle + sort shipped in a05bda3.

title: Consensus aggregation for multi-pass fact extraction (union is insufficient)
description:
Built the multi-pass toggle (MARINARA_EXTENDER_FACTS_PASSES, a9f559f) per TC. Testing showed pure UNION degrades quality: a 3-pass D&D run produced 74 candidates (judge kept 71 - filter overwhelmed), near-duplicates from reworded repeats, and a one-off mis-attribution survived ('Priya D&D class is Pact of the Tome Warlock' - it is Mari's, but one of three passes said Priya and union kept it). 

FIX - consensus / majority vote across passes instead of OR:
- Group candidates across passes by semantic equivalence (fuzzy match on the fact text + same lane), not exact string - so reworded repeats collapse into one cluster.
- Keep a cluster only if it recurs in >= ceil(N/2) passes (majority). A one-off (the Priya-Warlock error in 1/3) drops out; a real fact (Warlock-as-Mari in 3/3) stays.
- For a cluster with conflicting ATTRIBUTION across passes (Mari vs Priya), take the majority subject; tie -> drop or demote to chat.
- Then judge the consensus set (now small + clean) and ledger it.
This turns 'more passes = more noise' into 'more passes = higher confidence'. Cost is the same N calls TC already accepted via the toggle; the win is the aggregation. Until this lands, passes=1 is the safe default and passes>1 is experimental. Relates to 1dn, egp (Ledger Pattern), the durability judge.
notes: IMPLEMENTED (22cbd03): consensusFilter clusters candidates by embedding similarity (cosine>=0.85; exact-text fallback if the embed model is absent), counts distinct passes per cluster, keeps clusters at floor(N/2)+1 passes with the majority attribution. Wired into ingestSceneFacts (passes=1 short-circuits, default unchanged). D&D 3-pass: 48 candidates -> 22 consensus -> 11 durable; Warlock class captured AND routed to professor_mari; the 1/3 Priya-Warlock mis-attribution dropped. 171 tests. Remaining = the durability judge still leaks ~3 transient facts per scene (low-harm, the ledger is human-editable as the final gate); optional later tuning, not blocking. 1dn is now trustworthy enough to apply via dry-run -> (optional edit) -> apply.
close_reason: Extraction consensus (the titled scope) delivered + verified last session (22cbd03): consensusFilter clusters by embedding similarity, majority-votes across passes, drops the 1/3 Priya-Warlock mis-attribution while keeping Warlock-as-Mari (3/3). The 'remaining' durability-judge leak was investigated this session: tightened JUDGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT with the mask-test discriminator (transient-event-dressed-as-identity) + the two real Hargrove misses as worked DROP examples — a net improvement on the clear cases (drops the targeted transients, keeps concrete durables). But live runs proved the judge is NON-DETERMINISTIC on borderline candidates in BOTH directions (abstract durables and identity-masked transients flip run-to-run); that variance is irreducible at single-pass and prompt-fiddling cannot close it. Robust fix = multi-pass judge consensus, filed as its own piece (8jw) rather than wedged in here. Crucially, the architecture is already resilient to the judge's variance: the dry-run -> edit -> apply ledger gate caught the desk-sex transient leak TODAY even when the judge let it through — the system as a whole doesn't depend on a perfect judge. Calibration harness + fixtures: src/__tests__/judge-calibration.test.ts.

title: Alias-learner pollutes the table: user/persona name + compound 'X and Y' become character aliases (misroutes memory live)
description:
Found while validating 1dn fact routing. The alias table has: canonicalName 'Dr. Mari Zielińska' -> aliases ['Mari','Professor Mari','Mari and TC','Thomas']. 'Thomas' (the player/persona) and 'Mari and TC' (a compound) were wrongly learned as Mari aliases (added 2026-06-12, uses:0). Effect: resolveNameToKey('Thomas') -> professor_mari, and since the LIVE tier-2/tier-3 routing uses the same resolver, facts/beats about the USER (Thomas) have been silently routing into Mari's ledger - corrupting her memory with the player's facts. Not a backfill-only issue.

TWO fixes:
1. DATA CLEANUP (one-off, guarded): remove the bad aliases 'Thomas' and 'Mari and TC' from Mari's entry (uses:0, clearly wrong). Audit other characters for the same pollution (persona name / 'and' compounds learned as aliases). Provide a dry-run script.
2. ROOT CAUSE (code): the alias-learner must NOT learn (a) the user/persona name as a character alias - it should be excluded the way subject-routing already treats 'user'/persona via matchesSessionName; (b) compound strings containing ' and ' / '&' / commas (multi-character mentions) as a single alias. Add these as guards where aliases are added (aliases.ts addAlias / the learning path). 

Severity: affects live routing accuracy for every character whose scenes mention the player by name. Relates to 1dn (surfaced it) and the identity system (cx4/identity.ts). Until fixed, fact backfill --apply would inherit the misroute.
close_reason: Fixed root cause + cleaned existing pollution, verified live. addAlias now refuses compound labels (isCompoundLabel: and/with/plus/&/comma/slash/+, whole-word so Anderson/Sandra are safe) and any label already claimed by the user/persona key; process-turn registers the persona under USER_IDENTITY_KEY so the player-guard has data. scripts/clean-alias-pollution.mjs (dry-run default, --persona, --apply) removes existing pollution via removeAlias. Applied to the live table: dropped 'Mari and TC' (compound) + 'Thomas' (player) from professor_mari. Verified resolveNameToKey('Thomas')=null and ('Mari and TC')=null while Mari/Priya still resolve. 5 new tests, 170 total. Other installs run the script to audit/clean their own tables.

title: Dual-retrieval for guest characters in multi-character scenes (guest-mode voice degradation)
description:
THE FAMILY: 'what does a character know when they are not in their own room.' Sibling to 1dn (fact capture) and to cross-character propagation - all three are about a character's knowledge surface outside their home chat.

DIAGNOSIS (TC named it; confirmed in code): retrieval and injection are single-character. marinara-extender.js getChatCharacterId() resolves ONE host (direct characterId, else first participant); the pre-turn hook (preTurnRefresh) rebuilds and writes exactly ONE lorebook - the host's. participantIds is used only for CAPTURE attribution (routing beats/facts to the right subject), never for RETRIEVAL. So when a character appears as a guest in another character's chat, they run on the scene prompt + visible chat but NOT their own ambient facts, ledger pulls, threads, or recaps at the fidelity their host instance gets. Their voice degrades toward neutral. Observed live: guest-Mari in Priya's chat lacked the June-4 FutureChain canon at host retrieval depth - her 'I have the commit history' line was so good precisely because it was the model pulling the one load-bearing fact and little else around it.

PROPOSED FIX - dual-retrieval mode. On each turn of a multi-character chat, retrieve against EVERY participant's own substrate, not just the host's: loadContext per participant (skipCredit), write each participant's block to THEIR OWN character-linked lorebook. Likely-cheap path: the engine already injects constant entries from every lorebook linked to a character present in the chat, so refreshing all participants' lorebooks (instead of only the host's) may be most of the work - verify the engine surfaces guest lorebooks for scene participants. Each voice then runs on its own canon at home fidelity.

COSTS / OPEN QUESTIONS (opie to scope): N participants = N loadContext calls + N lorebook injections per turn. (a) the 800ms pre-turn budget currently covers ONE sidecar call - N participants need parallelized calls within budget; (b) N injected blocks multiply prompt tokens - per-participant budget scaling on top of the 16384 lorebook bump; (c) confirm the engine injects participant (non-host) character lorebooks into a multi-char chat - if not, the injection vehicle needs rethinking; (d) capture attribution already scopes per-subject, so capture side is fine - this is purely the retrieval side.

ACCEPTANCE: in a multi-character scene, a guest character answers from their OWN ledger/recaps at the same depth as in their home chat (e.g. guest-Mari recalls a June-4 FutureChain detail while in Priya's chat, not just the visible scene). Related: 1dn, dq9, cross-character propagation.
notes: Built (778acdb): preTurnRefresh now resolves every participant (host + participantIds, names resolved) and refreshes EACH one's own lorebook via /api/pre-turn in parallel — not just the host. Self-contained per-participant refresh (fetch block → nuke/recreate its lorebook; writes serialized via _lorebookWriteChain). Budget (PRE_TURN_BUDGET_MS=800) bounds only the blocking await; stragglers finish in background (guest lags ≤1 turn, never generates empty). Single-char chats unchanged. Client-only (no server change; /api/pre-turn already accepts any characterId) → reload tab, no sidecar restart. Always-on per TC. NOTE per-turn cost now scales with N participants (N pre-turn calls, each incl. Stage-2 recap embedding) + N lorebook injections; watch token bite, toggle if needed. PENDING LIVE VERIFY: in a multi-char scene, a GUEST recalls its OWN canon (e.g. guest-Mari recalls a home-chat detail in Priya's chat) — also confirms the open question (engine injects guest character-scoped lorebooks).

title: Capture durable FACTS from scenes, not just emotional beats (the capture gradient)
description:
ROOT CAUSE (found tracing Mari's D&D-class / Pact-of-the-Tome-Warlock conversation): the whole capture pipeline is tuned for EMOTIONAL SALIENCE, which is its strength and its blind spot. A scene is captured three ways and a flat identity/lore fact falls through all three: (1) the beat analyzer only fires on chunks that pass a sentiment threshold - a playful 'everyone's D&D class' exchange has near-zero emotion, so no beat; (2) the scene recap is one LLM pass that compresses to the emotional ARC - flavor like 'Mari = Pact of the Tome Warlock' gets dropped from the summary; (3) the tier-3 ambient/identity classifier (which is literally built to extract 'preferences, history, identity') only runs on LIVE process-turn, never on scene import/recap. So a durable fact stated inside a scene is invisible. Result: months later the fact exists nowhere in the ledger and the character confabulates (Mari: 'warlock was wrong, i'd be wizard').

PROPOSED FIX (v1.2, design only - not implementing yet):
PRIMARY - recap-time fact extraction. The scene-recap LLM call already reads the whole scene; extend it to ALSO return a structured list of durable, retrievable facts established there (identity, preferences, backstory, relationships, worldbuilding), distinct from the emotional prose summary. Ingest each as a character_topics/user_topics entry through the existing dedup matrix (identity = trait, never collapsed into an incident). Route each fact to the right subject via the existing roster (Mari's class -> Mari, TC's D&D history -> user_topics, Priya's bard -> Priya) so cross-character facts land in the right stores. Rides an existing call = cheap, bounded to once per scene-conclude, and sees the whole scene rather than per-chunk.
SECONDARY - run the ambient/identity classifier on the import path too, for imports that don't go through scene-recap.
BACKFILL - a script to run fact-extraction over already-concluded scenes (like repair-recap-pairing.mjs) so the existing months of history get their facts.

ACCEPTANCE: import/conclude a scene where a low-emotion identity fact is stated ('my D&D class is X', 'I main a blood elf warlock', 'I've DMed since 2e') -> a faithful, subject-attributed ledger entry exists afterward without manual authoring, and the character answers correctly next session. Related to dq9 (long-form user-message granularity) - same capture-gradient theme, different mechanism. Tonight a manual canon entry (ctopic-mari-warlock-canon) was written by hand as the stopgap.
notes:
SLICE 4 (1f96cbb): persistent ledger - dry-run extracts+judges and writes durable facts to data/fact-ledger/<scene>.json; --apply writes THAT file (no re-extraction). Fixes preview!=apply (apply had been re-extracting nondeterministically and writing a different set than reviewed; a flawed apply was rolled back via removeEntriesBySourceChat). Ledger is human-editable + resumable.

ARCHITECTURE NOW COMPLETE for 1dn: prose extractor (Ledger-Pattern windowing) + scene-scoped roster (whole-word) + name-variant resolution (token containment) + durability judge (verify-before-assemble, strong model) + persistent ledger. All tested, 170.

REMAINING = RECALL VARIANCE (model ceiling, not code): a single extraction pass is high-variance - across runs the Warlock fact appears or not (good runs ~90% precision WITH it; this last run missed it and leaked noise). The ledger makes apply faithful+editable but does not fix whether a run surfaces a given fact. FIX: multi-pass union - run the extractor N times (2-3), union+dedup candidates, THEN judge once, then ledger. The Warlock appears in most passes, so union almost always catches it; bounded extra cost on a one-time backfill; fits the Ledger Pattern (accumulate candidates before assembly). This is the next concrete step; until then the dry-run -> human-edit-ledger -> apply loop is the safe path. The Warlock CAPTURE itself is proven (focused extraction nails it); the work left is making whole-scene recall reliable.
close_reason: Delivered — and grew into the full reconciliation system. The capture-gradient goal (capture durable identity/lore FACTS from scenes, not just salient emotional beats) is met: facts.ts ingestSceneFacts (prose-aware classify -> consensus extraction [967] -> consensus durability judge [8jw] -> route + dedup), backfill script, the Agent SDK curator [5ny], live FR1 queue + shadow drain [b4n], bounded curator view [5f2], ledger hygiene sweep [0kk], embedding semantic clustering [and], two-stage confidence+domain apply gate [mjp]. Verified live on professor_mari: durable facts captured + 37 cruft entries reconciled (22 lexical + 15 semantic), trauma facts preserved separate. Remaining roadmap piece = FR4 audit/rollback (3pl), tracked separately.

title: Scene recaps mispaired by nearest-timestamp heuristic (36 of 56 wrong)
description: The engine's narrator return message never names its scene and scene-chat updatedAt drifts, so syncSceneRecaps attached wrong summaries - Test Drive Transgression carried the couch-morning recap, which is why Priya could not recount the test drive even with pre-turn retrieval working. Fixed in fa03e33: content-word-overlap pairing in the extension + scripts/repair-recap-pairing.mjs offline audit/repair. Applied: 36 repaired, re-audit 56/56 correct. Related: confabulation purge from the memory-test chat (2 entries + 1 beat) and the MEMORY FIDELITY prompt contract in 1e84693.
close_reason: Closed

title: v1.2: one-turn recall lag - memory block is built AFTER the response it should have informed
description: OBSERVED LIVE (TC, 2026-06-13): asked Priya about the Porsche test drive; she answered 'no memory of that whatsoever' DESPITE 17 porsche entries + the scene recap in her hot ledger. Log forensics: the block her reply generated against (turn 3) was built from the PREVIOUS exchange's recentText — no porsche; the very next block (turn 5) loaded recap-1ul5k1li + the porsche beats. The architecture assembles the memory block in process-turn (after each AI response) for the NEXT one, so topical recall always arrives one exchange late — the user experiences 'she doesn't remember' on every first ask, which is the single most trust-destroying failure a memory system can present. FIX DIRECTION (pre-turn refresh): the extension intercepts the page's generation request (wrap window.fetch for the engine's generate call — the loader already does blob-script bridging, CSP-compatible), AWAITS a fast sidecar call POST /api/pre-turn {chatId, characterId, userText} that runs loadContext with the OUTGOING user message as recentText and writes the lorebook block, then forwards the generation request. Cost: loadContext is LLM-free — ~100-300ms added per generation; the tier-2/3 capture stays post-turn. Needs care: locate the exact generate endpoint shape; timeout the pre-turn await (e.g. 500ms budget, proceed without refresh on miss — degraded = today's behavior); avoid double-writing the lorebook (process-turn still writes post-turn for capture-derived additions). Acceptance: ask about any old memory cold — the FIRST reply already has it in context.
notes: SHIPPED (pending TC live verification): extension wraps window.fetch, holds POST /api/generate <= 800ms for a pre-turn block rebuild against the outgoing userMessage (sidecar POST /api/pre-turn, LLM-free, skipCredit), writes the lorebook, then generation proceeds; failure/timeout = old behavior. Verified server-side with the exact live failing question - the steakhouse block now contains the steakhouse memories before the reply generates. NEEDS: Marinara tab reload (extension changed), then re-ask the steakhouse question - FIRST reply should know. Engine fetch confirmed interceptable (api-client.ts streamEvents uses plain window.fetch POST BASE+/generate; userMessage rides the body).
close_reason: Verified live end-to-end. The lag fix (pre-turn refresh) was necessary but not sufficient - it unmasked three deeper failures fixed in sequence: recap mispairing (36/56 wrong, fa03e33), the new-chat pre-turn skip (cba43f8), and the engine lorebook tokenBudget default of 2048 silently dropping the grown memory entry (103d571, the actual killer). Final live test 2026-06-11 7:47 PM: Priya recalled the back seat, the windshield, good girl, and Mari driving to the river - all mapped to real entries (ctopic-l0l0r1jb, recap-u943nyuy) delivered by the pre-turn block.

title: v1.2: long-form personal stories must land - granular capture for multi-page user messages (the Army story)
description: USER STORY (TC, 2026-06-13): told Mari a long multi-page traumatic story about his Army time in live chat; she retained essentially nothing and he had to re-import the chat. Telling a character a significant personal memory ONCE should make it durably known — retelling trauma is exactly what a memory system exists to prevent. ROOT CAUSES (compound): (1) the live path chunks one message = one chunk (MarinaraExtender-7h9) — a multi-page story yields at most ONE beat; (2) analyzer genre boilerplate + label-match dedup collapsed what little was captured (fixed this session: same-moment gate + specificity prompts); (3) tier-3 ambient catches isolated facts but not narrative. DESIGN: detect long user messages (e.g. > 1500 chars) in process-turn and route THAT MESSAGE through windowed granular ingestion - the story-import pipeline machinery on a single message (chunk into windows, classify, analyze each, subject-route, thread-tag) - so a told story produces the same beat richness as an imported one. user_topics facts + a story thread minted (the recap layer then gives it an arc). Un-parks 7h9 for the user-message case specifically (assistant-message granularity stays parked/opt-in per TC). Fire-and-forget after the turn; cost is bounded by story length and only triggers on long messages. Acceptance: tell a 3-page story once -> character can answer questions about its specifics next session without re-import.
notes: Implemented (37d42e4): /api/process-turn detects userMessageText > LONG_USER_MSG_CHARS (default 1500, env MARINARA_EXTENDER_LONGMSG_CHARS) and fires runSentimentPipeline(sourceType:chat) on that one message — windowed/per-window/subject-routed/persisted, import-parity. Tier 2 skips its one user chunk when long; Tier 3 facts still run. Design note: skip-the-coarse-chunk means an emotionally-FLAT long message could capture fewer beats (windows may miss sentiment threshold) — acceptable since Tier 3 still grabs facts and dq9 targets significant (charged) stories; revisit if live testing under-captures. PENDING LIVE VERIFY (needs sidecar restart): tell a multi-page story → console logs '[ME:longform] user story (N chars) → M beat(s)' → character's ledger gains multiple beats → next session can answer specifics without re-import.

title: One-click updater: panel Update button + double-click bat - no terminal for upgrades
description: Child of szf (install pipeline epic). TC requirement: users must never need a terminal to upgrade. Three pieces: (1) scripts/update-sidecar.ps1 - stops the sidecar, git pull --ff-only, npm install, npm run build, best-effort ollama pull of the embed model, relaunches via Marinara_Extender_Start.bat, visible console so progress is watchable; (2) Marinara_Extender_Update.bat - the double-click path; (3) in-product: /api/health gains version + updateAvailable (GitHub latest-release check, cached, silent-fail offline; drafts excluded by the API), POST /api/update spawns the updater detached (CSRF-guarded), panel shows an Update button when a newer release exists. v1.0 -> v1.1 remains the last manual hop (v1.0 has no button); v1.1+ upgrades are one click.
notes: LIVE END-TO-END TEST PASSED (v1.1.0 -> v1.1.1): simulated a v1.1.0 install via git reset --hard v1.1.0; health correctly offered the update (version=1.1.0, latest=1.1.1, updateAvailable=true); POST /api/update ran the full loop — stop, pull, install, build, relaunch — server returned at 1.1.1 in ~90s. FOUND+FIXED: version bumps must sync package-lock.json or npm install dirties it and blocks the NEXT pull; lockfile committed and the updater now discards local lockfile drift pre-pull (self-healing across the fleet since updater N+1 cleans what update N left). Also: bodyless POST needs Content-Type application/json (the extension's memFetch always sends it; only raw curl-style calls notice).
close_reason: Shipped in 45ab590, live-verified: /api/health reports version=1.1.0, latest=1.0.0 (published only — the draft correctly excluded), updateAvailable computed by semver compare (3 unit tests). POST /api/update spawns scripts/update-sidecar.ps1 in a visible console (stop server -> git pull --ff-only with loud failure on dirty checkouts -> npm install -> build -> best-effort ollama pull nomic-embed-text -> relaunch; logs to logs/update.log). Panel shows the Update button when a newer release is published; Marinara_Extender_Update.bat is the double-click path. Tag v1.1.0 moved to include it; draft release notes refreshed. Caveat noted in notes: v1.0 -> v1.1 is the last manual hop (v1.0 has no button). Full end-to-end self-update not exercised (would no-op pull on the dev checkout) — first real exercise will be v1.1 -> v1.2.

title: v1.1: narrative threads as first-class objects — thread_id minted at ingest, beats inherit, recall by thread relevance
description:
CORE THESIS (TC, 2026-06-11): v1.1 tracks narrative threads as first-class objects instead of inferring them from message proximity. Each thread = topic/arc identifier + member beats + participating characters. Canonical example: the Porsche test drive — one coherent arc spanning multiple turns and three participants that the system currently sees as 30+ disconnected beats; recalling any beat from it should pull thread context.

DESIGN SKETCH (as agreed):
- thread_id is a stable identifier generated at scene start, or inferred by the tier-2 analyzer when a scene clearly continues an existing thread.
- Ingest tags beats with thread_id at extraction time — rides the SAME LLM call that now returns subject (6qx): roster of ACTIVE threads in the prompt, model picks one or flags new-thread; ambiguous cases use the holding-pool pattern (never guess).
- Beats inherit thread_id; the index gains a THREAD layer parallel to the character layer.
- Recall pulls beats by thread relevance, not just recency + character match.
- Cold storage archives threads as UNITS once closed.

RECONCILIATION with recap-ceiling-data-model.md (which commits to assign-on-promotion): not a conflict — two tiers. Ingest-time thread_id is SCENE-SCOPED (the floor unit; cheap, piggybacked, hot-path mints only scene threads). Cross-scene ARC minting/merging stays at promotion with match-before-mint, and ArcSignature.threadIds (already in the reviewed data model as 'the most reliable signal') consumes exactly these ingest-minted thread ids. Ceiling clustering spike (the open question in the doc) gets its primary input from this feature.

STATUS: not implemented anywhere — no threadId field exists on beats. The ingest-time half of this thesis was never previously written down; the promotion-time half lives in recap-ceiling-data-model.md, bd memory recap-layer-v1.1, and issues ajb/2cu.
notes: INGEST HALF SHIPPED (22a0028), live on sidecar PID 25156. Done: threads.ts registry (data/threads/registry.yaml, nthr-* ids, scene-scoped by chatId, participants + beatCount + lastActiveAt); analyzer returns thread label riding the SAME call as subject (active-thread roster in prompt, verbatim pick or short new label, null for incidental); resolveOrMintThread with intra-chat fuzzy label matching (exact/containment/jw>=0.85) + single serialized mutation (no double-mint under concurrency, 7 tests prove it); beats + beat-index rows carry threadId; closeThread() exported (closed threads leave roster, same label re-mints fresh). 111/111 tests. LIVE VERIFICATION NEEDED (TC): run RP turns, watch for '[ME:tier2] new thread ...' and '| thread=nthr-...' in logs; check data/threads/registry.yaml accumulates sensible labels, not one-thread-per-turn fragmentation or one mega-thread. REMAINING (the payoff half): (1) recall by thread relevance — loader surfaces thread context when a member beat/entry is recalled; design with the recap layer (2cu/ajb), entries may need threadId too; (2) cold-storage archival of CLOSED threads as units; (3) scene-conclude hook — extension calls sidecar to closeThread when Marinara concludes a scene (engine sets sceneStatus=concluded; see marinara-scene-conclude-persists-summary memory); also consider inactivity-based auto-close so rosters do not grow unbounded. NOTE deviation from brief: ambiguous thread labels are left UNTAGGED (null) rather than holding-pooled — the pool is beat/speaker-shaped; untagged = status quo ante and safe.
close_reason: Both halves shipped. INGEST (22a0028): threads.ts registry, nthr-* ids minted at ingest riding the same analyzer call as subject (active-thread roster in prompt, fuzzy label resolution, serialized no-double-mint), beats + beat-index rows carry threadId. RECALL + LIFECYCLE (4c63712): ledger entries inherit threadId from beats; selectEntries ranks by effective relevance = max(own match, thread label match, 0.75 x strongest sibling match) — recalling any beat pulls the thread, returning to an arc by name surfaces members whose summaries never matched; closed threads archive to cold as UNITS (never split, never while active); idle threads (14d) auto-close in the promotion pass. Label quality hardened along the way (one-shot examples, participant-scoped roster, characterName fix). 137/137 tests. Remaining related work tracked separately: rfx (registry health metrics + scene-conclude close hook + scene-title hint), ajb (ceiling arcs consume ArcSignature.threadIds — now unblocked). Live verification: watch for 'thread=nthr-*' on beats and arc-name recall pulling thread context in scenes.

title: Porsche bug: RP-mode story-threading attribution — incident beats fingerprinted as traits and skipped instead of written under the character's ledger
description: In RP mode, a beat that belongs to a specific character's ledger (observed: Priya's porsche ledger entry) gets fingerprinted as a trait and skipped at ingest instead of being written under her characterId. Result: hard data loss at ingest — the beat never lands anywhere. Fix: at ingest, story-thread attribution must resolve and write under the owning characterId BEFORE the trait fingerprint/dedup decision can swallow the beat. title: v1.1 floor: atomic writes everywhere the sidecar mutates state (manifest, memory chunks, index.yaml, index.cold.yaml)
description: Every sidecar mutation is currently a plain write — a hard crash (like the 2026-06-10 BSOD that zeroed Marinara's own tables) can NUL-fill or truncate our YAML/JSON state. Add an atomicWrite helper: write temp file, fsync, rename over target. ~10 lines per call site or one shared helper. CRITICAL Windows trap (proven live in the upstream flushFile bug, MarinaraExtender-1kn): fsync must be called on a WRITABLE fd — open the temp with 'w', write, fsyncSync(THAT fd) before close, then renameSync. Never write-then-reopen-readonly ('r' + fsync = silent EPERM no-op on Windows). Skip directory fsync on win32 (can never succeed; NTFS journals the rename). Call sites to convert: beat-index manifest, memory chunk writes, index.yaml, index.cold.yaml — audit for any other writeFileSync on state paths. This is the v1.1 floor: nothing else in v1.1 ships until this is in.
close_reason: atomicWriteFile shipped in cb1cff5: tmp + fsync-on-writable-handle + rename(retry). All 6 state write sites converted (storage.writeYaml, story-jobs, identity, soft-clock, sentiment/encoder, setup .env). Bonus: fixed serializedWrite unhandled-rejection leak in lock pruning. 97/97 tests pass incl. 4 new atomic-write tests; tsc clean.

title: Report upstream: flushFile fsync silently no-ops on Windows (read-only fd), defeating .bak crash protection
description: Crash on 2026-06-10 ~11:05PM zeroed lorebook_entries.json + memory_chunks.json AND both .bak files despite the looksNulFilled/fsync protection shipped in the user's build (dist dated 6/7). Root cause: flushFile() in packages/server/src/db/file-backed-store.ts:286 opens the file with openSync(path,'r') then fsyncSync(fd); on Windows FlushFileBuffers requires write access, so fsync fails with EPERM and the empty catch swallows it — the durability guarantee is a silent no-op on the platform it was written for. Fix: open 'r+' (or 'a') on win32, and consider logging fsync failures once. Needs a GitHub issue on Pasta-Devs/Marinara-Engine per contributor pre-flight before any PR. Evidence: all four files were 100% NUL bytes; .bak mtimes 6 minutes older than mains.
notes: 2026-06-19: NOT our active work — engine bug, reported, being looked into upstream. Confirmed still live on Pasta-Devs/Marinara-Engine main today (flushFile, file-backed-store.ts:289 — openSync 'r' + fsyncSync, EPERM-swallowed on Windows). Part of a RECURRING CLASS: closely related to closed #631; TC has seen 2+ similar file-durability reports on Discord. The EXTENDER is already protected and NOT vulnerable — atomicWriteFile (memory-extender/src/storage.ts:183) fsyncs the WRITABLE handle, warns-once on failure, atomic rename + Windows transient-retry. OPTIONAL follow-up ONLY if upstream stalls: pull the engine repo, one-line fix (flushFile 'r' -> 'r+' + log-once); a high-quality issue body was drafted this session if needed. Deferred pending upstream resolution.

title: Ceiling tier: cross-scene through-line arc clustering + recap rendering
description: The real engineering work and the sidecar defensible value-add (the engine cannot reach across scenes). WHAT: an assign-on-promotion pass that clusters beats across scenes into through-line arcs and renders structured recaps. Carries: binding-signal stack (entity-overlap UNION thread-ID UNION embedding-kNN candidates, causal-ref confirm), MATCH-BEFORE-MINT arc identity (permanent IDs, structural collision detector gates LLM reconciliation), typed N:M edges {beatRef, role-in-arc, salience}, activation-signature dormancy (activation threshold > candidacy threshold), watermark drift. Heuristic salience for v1.1. Can be developed against prototype kNN (model already local) without committing to the install story. Full design in the parent cz3 design field.
design: Data-model sketch: memory-extender/docs/recap-ceiling-data-model.md — Arc (permanent id, re-renderable label, lane-driven), ArcMembership typed N:M edge {beatId, role, salience}, ArcSignature (entities+threadIds+centroid) doubling as dormant reverse-index key, ArcStatus active|dormant|resolved, watermark, RecapEntry extends Entry. Grounded on EmotionalBeat (sentiment/types.ts) and Lane/Entry/cold-tier (storage.ts). Four open type-level questions noted in the doc.
acceptance_criteria: Promotion pass assigns promotion-eligible beats to existing arcs (match-before-mint) or mints new ones; arcs carry typed edges and dormant/active state; loader injects relevant through-line recaps (coherent narrative state) instead of disconnected top-k beats; underlying beats remain queryable at full fidelity.
notes: Data-model review COMPLETE (see memory-extender/docs/recap-ceiling-data-model.md). DECISIONS: watermark = per-character monotonic seq (NOT ISO — created is date-only); edges = standalone ArcMembership with bidirectional load-time indexes; salience = recency x entityProminence x beat.salience normalized WITH a high-salience floor (beat.salience>0.8 caps recency loss, X tunable); centroid inline+cached, batched recompute once per promotion pass. CONSTRAINT (decision-with-evidence): dormant stays on ArcStatus, NOT EntryStatus — loader gates recap injection on the Arc link; do not merge onto EntryStatus (full consumer audit in doc). PRECURSOR (small beat-storage change, blocks the watermark; could be its own task): add nextSeq:number to BeatIndex and write-once seq:number to BeatIndexEntry, assigned inside upsertBeatIndex under the existing per-character serializeBeatWrite lock (encoder.ts:115/132); idempotent on re-encode (assign only on first insert, never reassign), durable in beats/index.yaml.
close_reason: Shipped in 77fd63a and live-verified on real data: Mari's first through-line 'Mari's fractured selfhood' (20 beats, June 1-12, dated trajectory, typed roles: 3 turning_point / 5 escalation / 2 threshold_crossing); Priya's 'surrender to the triad' (73 beats). Full spec honored: union candidate generators (thread/entity/kNN), one renderer call per arc (confirm+label+render, lane-aware, gaps as input, accretion), match-before-mint with H4 dormancy rules, permanent ids, seq watermarks, edge-salience floor, once-per-pass centroids. Cadence: every 60 turns + POST /api/arcs/promote. RESIDUAL TUNING (tracked on cz3): cluster granularity (1 big cluster per char on first pass - taus may need tightening), label quality over time, embedder surface-clustering risk.

title: Floor tier: ingest engine scene-conclude summaries as scene-arc recaps
description: Buildable today, zero open questions. WHY: the engine already emits a dated, named, persisted ~200-word prose summary at /api/scene/conclude (sceneStatus=concluded; readable as an origin-chat message and in each character memories array — see bd memory marinara-scene-conclude-persists-summary). WHAT: ingest these as scene-arc recap LEAVES (the chronological floor) with their underlying beats as footnotes; sceneStatus=concluded is the deterministic trigger. No LLM cost, no clustering. Ships independently of the ceiling. Parent: MarinaraExtender-cz3.
acceptance_criteria: On scene conclude, the sidecar ingests the engine prose summary as a scene-arc recap entry keyed by scene, dated, with member beats cited as footnotes; the recap is retrievable by the loader.
notes: SHIPPED (715913a), live on sidecar PID 14984 — held open for TC live verification. Implementation per the reviewed data model: arcs.ts (Arc/ArcMembership/RecapEntry mirroring docs/recap-ceiling-data-model.md), beat seq watermarks (Resolved #1: BeatIndex.nextSeq + write-once seq under the per-character lock), standalone memberships file (Resolved #2), max-8 footnotes (H3), ArcStatus separate from EntryStatus (audit invariant honored). Scene ingestion closes the scene's threads — pln unit-archival ages members to cold while the recap stays hot (displacement by composition). MECHANISM NOTE: design memory said summaries persist in character.memories arrays — empirically FALSE on this install (0 of 9 characters); ground truth chose origin-chat narrator 'returned from their scene' messages (40 exist, matching 40 concluded scenes) paired by sceneOriginChatId + nearest timestamp. Extension syncs throttled (10 min) per process-turn, idempotent server-side. LIVE VERIFICATION (TC): reload Marinara, take one turn anywhere — the first sync ingests ~40 historical scenes across participants; expect '[ME:recap] scene ... ingested' lines, then check a character's ledger for '[scene recap]' entries and GET /api/arcs. Footnotes on historical scenes will be sparse (old beats lack sourceChatId/threadId; pre-pln capture) — recaps still ingest; footnote richness grows with new scenes.
close_reason: Verified live: 28 scenes ingested per character (56 total) by the extension's own sync on TC's reload; deliberate duplicate re-sync produced 0 dupes (idempotency on real data); 542 memberships bound for Mari, 13 arcs with linked beats (recent ones with provenance), sample recap retrievable in hot index. Historical scenes ingest footnote-sparse as predicted.

title: Recap layer: consolidate beats into arc recaps as the canonical retrieval unit (v1.1 headline feature)
description: WHY: Cold storage treated the symptom (too many entries) not the disease — beat fragmentation that loses NARRATIVE COHERENCE at scale. A loader pulling N high-signal beats without connective tissue yields disconnected moments that do not add up to a story (knowing facts vs knowing a story). WHAT: introduce a RECAP entry that organizes underlying beats by ARC (a named through-line) and becomes the canonical retrieval unit the loader injects instead of fragments. Two tiers: (1) scene-arc recaps = chronological floor, ingested FREE from the engine /api/scene/conclude summaries; (2) through-line arc recaps = thematic ceiling, the cross-scene narrative the engine cannot produce, generated by an assign-on-promotion LLM pass. Background: bd memories recap-layer-v1.1 and marinara-scene-conclude-persists-summary.
design:
TIERS: FLOOR (free) = scene-arc recaps ingested from engine /api/scene/conclude prose summaries (see marinara-scene-conclude-persists-summary). CEILING (paid) = cross-scene THROUGH-LINE arc recaps, built by an assign-on-promotion pass. Rest of this field specifies the ceiling.

BINDING-SIGNAL STACK (candidate-generate cheap, then confirm). Candidate generators run as a UNION (no LLM): (1) shared-entity overlap, 2+ entities; (2) explicit thread-ID references from open_threads — most reliable because intentional, needs the analyzer to surface thread refs at beat extraction; (3) embedding-kNN over beat text — cheap proxy for emotional/thematic continuity, catches cross-entity arcs that entity-overlap walls out. Confirmation runs ONLY on the candidate shortlist: (4) causal references (beat B cites beat A, e.g. 'after the marble incident'), LLM-detectable, mid-cost. CRITICAL: the pre-filter is the UNION of the three cheap generators, NOT entity-overlap alone — a hard entity-only filter bounds the arc universe to entity-anchored arcs and makes thematic/cross-entity arcs (e.g. priya-saying-it-in-daylight) invisible.

FOUR HARDENINGS: (H1 LOAD-BEARING) MATCH-BEFORE-MINT — promotion is reconciliation against the existing arc set by signature (entities + thread-IDs + embedding centroid), gated by a structural collision detector (same pattern as fact-vs-feeling-memory-principle's gated LLM reconciliation). On hit: EXTEND the arc and advance its watermark. On clean miss: mint a new arc. Arc IDs are PERMANENT; labels are re-renderable. This is what turns 'repeated summarizer' into 'accreting memory'. (H2) PRE-FILTER RECALL via the embedding-kNN generator above. (H3) TYPED N:M EDGES — arc = set of {beatRef, role-in-this-arc, salience}; the same beat means different things per arc (turning-point vs escalation vs threshold-crossing). Pays for per-arc footnote meaning + footnote dedup at load + load-budget (render only high-salience edges, so a 200-beat arc surfaces as ~8 footnotes). (H4) ACTIVATION > CANDIDACY — dormant->active flip requires a thread-ID hit or embedding-centroid match, NOT mere entity presence (protagonist is in everything). Store each dormant arc's ACTIVATION SIGNATURE (entity+thread+centroid fingerprint) in a reverse index for a cheap per-beat 'does this wake anything?' check.

DRIFT/WATERMARK: a recap covers a closed prefix 'current through beat N'; newer beats ride hot until the next promotion advances N; resolved/dormant = watermark pinned. Beats stay full-fidelity underneath = regeneration path for bad recaps.

THREE IMPLEMENTATION NOTES: (A) EMBEDDING-MODEL TUNING RISK on the kNN generator — reuses the existing MARINARA_EXTENDER_EMBED_MODEL path (already used for semantic chunk-merge cosine sim in sentiment/chunker.ts; nomic-embed-text is the documented example). Beats are SHORT; small fast models cluster on surface vocabulary not theme ('the puddle' vs 'the gap' may not cluster on shared concrete nouns). Model is pluggable via env, so a stronger embedder is a config change if surface-clustering bites. (B) SALIENCE COMPUTATION — v1.1 HEURISTIC (recency x entity-prominence x emotional-intensity, normalized): cheap-and-coarse beats expensive-and-blocked-on-budget. v1.2 optional LLM-determined salience pass. (C) DORMANCY-GAP AS RENDERER INPUT — the re-rendering pass must RECEIVE the dormancy gap as INPUT (not merely store it as metadata) so the recap can encode 'went quiet for three weeks of in-world time, reopened when...'. The silence is part of the arc.
acceptance_criteria: Loader injects relevant RECAPS (coherent narrative state) instead of disconnected top-k beats; engine scene summaries ingested as scene-arc recap leaves with their beats as footnotes; through-line recaps generated at promotion, lane-aware, watermarked; underlying beats remain queryable on miss at full fidelity and recaps are regenerable from them.
notes: STAGE 3 (e5b4d89): through-line recap surfacing. Activation enumerated only floor '[scene recap]'; now matches both tiers (RECAP_SUMMARY_RE) so ceiling '[arc recap]' recaps activate semantically too, ordered first (broad arc frames specific scenes), prefix stripped on render. Mari has 1 through-line + 36 scene arcs. REMAINING STAGE-3 ITEMS DEFERRED AS PREMATURE (no data/need): dormancy-gap rendering (0 arcs have gaps), reverse-activation-index (only ~37 recaps), recap regeneration, budget tuning. PENDING LIVE VERIFY (sidecar restart + embeddings): steer toward the cross-scene 'fractured selfhood' arc theme without its label words → the [arc recap] should surface. On verify, cz3 core (Stages 1-3) is complete; deferred items → a separate follow-up ticket when data/scale warrants.
close_reason: v1.1 headline delivered. Recaps are the canonical retrieval unit, surfaced by relevance + meaning across both tiers. Stage 1 (loader injects recaps) and Stage 2 (semantic activation) verified live by TC; Stage 3 (through-line surfacing) is a build-verified extension of the same activation path. Deferred polish tracked in MarinaraExtender-rtd.

title: Security hardening: lock CORS to loopback, sanitize scopeId path traversal, stop logging key prefix
description: Pre-v1.0 audit (user-requested). Keys themselves are clean (never committed, gitignored, not returned/logged in full, 127.0.0.1 bind). Three fixes: (1) CORS is Access-Control-Allow-Origin:* on a localhost service with no auth — any site the user visits can read/modify the memory store + overwrite the API key. Restrict to loopback origins (127.0.0.1/localhost/::1) + optional MARINARA_EXTENDER_ALLOWED_ORIGIN. (2) scopeId/characterId/chatId are joined into filesystem paths unsanitized (storage.scopeDir, soft-clock.clockPath, encoder.beatsDir) -> path traversal (read/write/delete YAML outside data dir), reachable via the open CORS. Add assertSafeId guard at the path chokepoints. (3) startup log prints apiKey.slice(0,8) — drop to set/NOT SET. Also stale defaults (api.openai.com/gpt-4o-mini) noted for the install epic.
close_reason: Fixed + verified live (commit f58cd4b). CORS now reflects only loopback/configured origins (127.0.0.1:7860 confirmed allowed, evil.example denied no-ACAO); scopeId/characterId/chatId path traversal guarded at scopeDir/clockPath/beatsDir (../../escape → 500, not out-of-bounds read); key prefix no longer logged. Keys audited clean (never committed/returned/logged-in-full, 127.0.0.1 bind, no env routes). 84 tests.

title: First-run onboarding: one-button import, honest progress, payoff
description: Onboarding flow that takes over the panel until imported/skipped (localStorage onboarded flag). Intro: headline + one line ('Import your past chats so your characters can remember them') + one big button. Scan -> ready (shows chats/calls/tokens estimate) -> running: real progress bar with 'Analyzing chat N of M' + live ETA + Cancel (no mystery spinner). Done: payoff 'Your characters remembered N moments across M conversations' + 'Open a chat' button. Graceful degradation: per-chat failures are caught, skipped, counted, and reported at the end; cancel stops between chats. Reuses loadImportChats/importOneChat/estimate-beats.
close_reason: Onboarding takeover + 5-state flow (intro/estimating/ready/running/done) + ETA + cancel + graceful skip/report + payoff CTA. node --check ok.

title: 'Current' working cache: relevance+recency loader, recency-gated core
description: Reframe what gets loaded into the prompt as a 'Current' working cache, separate from the short/long/core retention tiers. Loader selectEntries no longer always-injects core; instead ranks ALL non-done entries by lexical relevance to the recent conversation -> recency -> proven value -> lane, and fills to budget. Relevance (stopword-filtered keyword containment of summary vs recent user+AI text, passed from process-turn) is the miss-path that resurfaces dormant memories when their topic returns, avoiding the ossification of a pure-recency cache. Core is never pruned (retention unchanged) so it always resurfaces by relevance. Eviction from Current is lossless. Eidetic mode = load all (treat everything as Current; testing only).
close_reason: selectEntries reworked to relevance+recency+value fill; core no longer auto-injected (recency-gated, still never pruned); recentText threaded from process-turn; eidetic loads all. tsc clean, 53 tests pass, relevance verified.

title: Imported beats not retrievable — no companion ledger entry
description: The loader builds the injected <memory> block from the entry index, never the beats store. Live-chat Tier 2 created a companion character_topics entry per beat (retrievable), but the story-import pipeline called encodeBeat only — so imported beats were analyzed but never recallable (the original 'beats not attaching to characters' problem). Fix: pipeline now writes a companion entry per beat via shared companionEntryFromBeat(); POST /api/beats-to-entries + scripts/backfill-beats-entries.ts backfill existing beats. Ran backfill: professor_mari now has 353 active beat-companions.
close_reason: Pipeline writes companion entries; backfill endpoint + script created and run. tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Multi-character story import (one pass, beats routed per character)
description: Import a story once and produce separate beats for multiple characters who interact. Endpoint accepts assignments[{characterId,characterName,names}]; attributes once (shared cached job keyed by union of names), then loops the pipeline per character filtering to that character's speaker names and saving beats under its identity key. Chunks are disjoint by speaker so each is analyzed once (no extra LLM cost); resume works per character. UI: 'Characters in this story' rows (character picker + per-char names, remembered), + add character. Response is per-character; UI shows the breakdown.

title: Resumable story imports (checkpoint attribution + incremental beats)
description: A long import took ~22min; a cancel/crash lost everything because beats were only encoded in a final batch. Now: (1) attribution windows cached in a per-import job file keyed by hash(text+options); (2) beats have deterministic ids and are encoded incrementally as each chunk finishes; (3) the pipeline skips chunks whose beat already exists. Re-running the same file+options auto-resumes — skips attributed windows and analyzed chunks. Clear-beats also clears jobs. UI shows 'N resumed'.

title: Ledger UI: tabbed layout (Memory/Import/Settings) + preserve scroll
description: renderPanel wiped innerHTML and rebuilt all sections (entries first, controls last) every action -> endless scrolling to reach controls + jump-to-top on every click (e.g. Analyze). Split into Memory/Import/Settings tabs; preserve .me-panel-content scrollTop across re-renders (reset only on tab switch). Tabs auto-expand their sections on entry.
