title: Automated capture tiers (1/2/3) bypass dedup — duplicates regenerate
description: Only the manual [remember:] and ingest-commands paths call isDuplicate. Tier 1 (digest.ts createEntry / snapshotSession every 30 min), Tier 2 (sentiment, api.ts ~555), and Tier 3 (ambient, api.ts ~591) all call writeEntry+upsertIndexEntry directly with no dedup. This continuously regenerates the exact duplicates runCleanup removes — Tier 3 ambient re-saves stable facts ('user grew up in Texas') every time the topic resurfaces. Fix: shared createEntryIfUnique(scope,scopeId,{lane,summary,content,...}) that reads the index, filters by lane, checks isDuplicate, writes only if unique. Route all three tiers through it.
close_reason: Added shared dedup.ts (createEntryIfUnique + isDuplicate); routed Tier 1 (digest/snapshot), Tier 2 (sentiment), Tier 3 (ambient) through it. api.ts now imports the single isDuplicate. tsc clean, 49 tests pass.

title: Conversational soft clock: temporal context inference
description:
Why: Characters have no concept of time of day or day of week. Memories feel decontextualized — there is no way to know if something happened in the morning after a hard week or late at night when someone could not sleep. Temporal context makes memories feel like real experiences not just facts.

What: Per-chat soft-clock.yaml state file tracking inferred timeOfDay, dayOfWeek, lastSessionEndedAt. Keyword signal detector (good morning → morning, just got home → evening, can't sleep → late night, happy friday → friday, etc). Morning-after logic: if previous session ended evening/night and new session opens with morning signal, infer a day has passed and update day-of-week. Inject current session context into memory block header so character knows roughly when they are. Enrich all encoded memories with timeContext field.
notes: No LLM needed — pure keyword matching. State stored per chatId. Feeds into all three capture tiers.
close_reason: Closed

title: Memory tier system: fields, loader, promotion/demotion engine
description:
Why: The current flat store treats a one-off mention the same as a deeply recurring theme. Memories need lifecycle management so important things persist and noise falls away naturally.

What: Add tier fields to Entry/IndexEntry (short|long|core|secondary_core), retrievalCount, recitationCount, cycleCount, lastRetrievedTurn. Update loader to always surface core/secondary_core before budget, short/long within budget. Add promotion engine (score >= 5 short→long, score >= 25 long→core) and demotion (not retrieved in 100 turns long→short, 50 turns short→pruned). Cycle counter: 3 promotion-demotion pairs → secondary_core regardless of tier.
notes: Score formula: retrievalCount + (recitationCount × 3). Demotion is time-based not score rollback. Core and secondary_core are never pruned.
close_reason: Closed

title: [feature] Delete routes to cold (recoverable); permanent purge requires digging
description:
User decision 2026-06-19: deleting a memory from the ledger must NOT hard-delete. Route it to the cold tier (recoverable, like supersession/FR2 and the beats recoverable-bucket), surface it in a 'Recently deleted' view with Restore, and make PERMANENT removal require digging into that view + a deliberate confirm — never a top-level action. User is fine having to dig to truly purge an actual memory; floated an optional 'deep cold' tier below cold as an extra buffer (parked unless cold+guarded-purge proves insufficient).

CURRENT: DELETE /api/entries/:id hard-deletes (deleteEntryFile + removeIndexEntry, api.ts:335). Two-step UI delete guard already added (marinara-extender.js, uncommitted). Cold infra exists: moveToCold/promoteFromCold/readColdIndex + the Retired view (character scope, listRetired filters supersededBy).

PLAN: user deletes are CHAT scope (removeEntry uses scope=chat) — keep recoverable-delete chat-scoped, separate from the character-scope Retired/reconciliation view. (1) soft-delete = moveToCold + a deletedAt marker (distinguishes user-deleted from superseded, which carries supersededBy); (2) 'Recently deleted (recoverable)' collapsible section in the memory tab, Restore = promoteFromCold + clear deletedAt; (3) permanent purge (the existing hard delete) only reachable from inside that section, behind its own confirm = the 'dig'. Update the two-step warning copy: delete is now recoverable, not 'character will no longer recall it forever'.
notes: Built + server-tested (236 tests, +7 in deleted.test.ts: soft-delete->cold, listDeleted excludes superseded, restore clears deletedAt + refuses superseded, purge removes file+row). loader.ts cold-recall skips deletedAt. api: DELETE soft by default, ?purge=true hard; GET /api/deleted; POST /api/entries/:id/restore (chat scope). UI: 'Recently deleted (recoverable)' section + two-step lane delete guard + two-step erase 'dig'. NOT browser-verified yet — needs sidecar RESTART (new server code/endpoints) + tab reload. Deep-cold tier parked (cold + two-step erase satisfies 'dig to purge'). Commit 3fe4e10.
close_reason: Verified in-browser 2026-06-19: delete→cold, restore (rejoins lane), and permanent erase (the dig) all confirmed working by the user. memFetch bodyless-400 fixed, delete repaints instantly.

title: [feature] Ambient facts should auto-expire after a period (time-based decay, not just usage tiering)
description:
The ledger fills with auto-extracted ambient facts — ambient.ts/facts.ts format them as '[about: <subject>] <fact>' into user_topics/character_topics (subject = user/TC or the character). Many are soft, low-durability meta-observations scraped from conversation (e.g. 'the character swears, makes jokes', 'occasionally guesses', 'designed to understand'), and they accumulate indefinitely. User flagged on 2026-06-19: these need to AUTO-CLEAR after a period of time.

CURRENT STATE: there is usage-based tiering (storage.ts: score = retrievalCount + recitationCount*3, hot/cold thresholds) but NO time-based expiry. Critically, the flagged entries have HIGH retrieval counts (~100+), so a usage-based policy will never clear them — the user wants them gone on a timer regardless of how often they match. Entries carry lastAccessed + lastRetrievedAt (ISO) and the supersession/cold machinery (moveToCold/restoreSupersededEntry, 3pl) already exists; there is no createdAt field yet (age-since-creation would need one, or derive from the entry file / id).

DESIGN DECISION PENDING (asking the user): (1) trigger basis — age since created vs age since last retrieved vs old-AND-rarely-used; (2) scope — ambient facts only vs all entries; (3) removal — move to cold (recoverable, consistent with rollback) vs delete. Duration is a tunable param (proposed default ~30 days). Implementation should reuse the cold tier, not hard-delete, to stay consistent with the FR2/FR4 'tier, never destroy' ethos.

title: [bug] Version string carries no build code — vdev/dev/ZIP builds are unidentifiable
description:
The panel shows 'extension vdev · server v1.1.5' with NO build code, so you cannot tell from the UI which build is actually loaded — which is exactly how a stale tab runs old code invisibly (setup.ts already carries a comment: a shipped fix 'didn't work' because it was never loaded; and it bit us live on 2026-06-19 testing 3pl). GROUNDED ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): buildVersion() (update.ts:46) gates the git sha behind existsSync(join(PKG_ROOT, '.git')), but PKG_ROOT = memory-extender/ and .git lives at the REPO ROOT (PKG_ROOT/..). memory-extender/.git does not exist, so the guard is always false in a normal checkout, git is never run, and the version string is bare currentVersion() (no +sha). Separately, non-git/ZIP installs (8u3) have no sha source at all.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERION (hard gate — ticket does NOT close without it): vdev/dev builds MUST include a short git hash OR a content hash in the version string shown in the panel. Specifically:
- A git checkout: version string includes the short HEAD sha (fix the .git guard to find the repo root, e.g. check PKG_ROOT and PKG_ROOT/.., or resolve upward).
- No .git (ZIP/release): version string includes a short CONTENT HASH of the served marinara-extender.js (the file the panel is actually running).
- The 'vdev'/unstamped marker must ALSO carry a code — never a bare 'vdev'.
- VERIFY: the panel's 'extension <ver>' line shows a hash for BOTH a git checkout and a no-.git install. If either shows a bare version with no code, this ticket stays open.

Relates to 8u3 (non-git updater). Propagation is free: setup.ts already stamps the served extension via __ME_VERSION__ -> buildVersion(), so fixing buildVersion surfaces the code in the panel automatically.
notes: Git-checkout half VERIFIED: panel header shows 'Marinara Extender v1.1.5+600e8a8' (the hash) instead of vdev. No-.git/ZIP content-hash half still unverified (needs a release/ZIP install to test). Stays open per the hard AC (both must show a code).

title: Ledger hygiene sweep — curator reconciles existing intra-ledger fact duplicates
description:
The live curator (b4n) only catches NEW facts colliding at the door; EXISTING intra-ledger duplicates — accumulated before reconciliation existed and from the force-creates — are never reviewed. Live examples on professor_mari: two Warlock entries (ctopic-auq9mre9 + canonical ctopic-bbupy8fl), and 'sold a solution to someone' / 'sold a fix to someone' pairs. A hygiene sweep clusters EXISTING active FACT entries by lexical similarity (jaccard, reusing selectLedgerView's measure) and hands each multi-entry cluster to the curator.

DESIGN (decided from measured data, not guessed): CLUSTER-AT-ONCE, not pairwise. The curator sees all N entries of a cluster in ONE call and picks the canonical + supersedes the rest (or rules them DISTINCT). Pairwise ('is A better than B') loses the 'which of N is most canonical' signal — same reason 8jw chose whole-context over fragmented. The empirical gate that makes this safe: on Mari's 1309-FACT ledger (incidents/threads excluded — those boilerplate-cluster to 168-895 and are NOT the curator's domain), MAX fact-cluster size is 4 (mostly pairs/triples) across thresholds 0.4-0.6. So cluster-at-once is a 2-4-entry call, never 50. Defensive cap (skip/flag clusters > ~12) as a guardrail current data never hits.

SCOPE: fact lanes only (character_topics traits + user_topics); exclude incidents (looksIncident / '[emotion]' summaries) + open_threads. THRESHOLD ~0.5 default (26 clusters on Mari, clean), configurable; the curator is the precision gate (can rule all-distinct), so threshold trades recall vs cost (#clusters = #curator calls). REUSE: supersedeEntry (FR2) + the shadow-first/audit/confidence machinery (b4n); runs offline like backfill. NEW: a cluster-adjudication curator interaction (cluster -> {canonical, redundant[], or all-distinct}) distinct from candidate-vs-ledger. SHADOW-first: log proposed supersessions, apply nothing; --apply executes. Relates to b4n, 2r3, 3pl (FR4 audit/rollback).
close_reason: Built + verified live on professor_mari. clusterFacts (union-find, jaccard, fact-scoped, oversized-cap) + clusterCurator (cluster-at-once adjudication: merge w/ canonical+redundant, or distinct, + confidence) + sweepLedger (shadow-first, separate sweep-audit.jsonl, --apply supersedes redundant by canonical via FR2) + scripts/reconcile-sweep.mjs. Live proof: 26 fact clusters @0.5 (max 4, matching the pre-build measurement); the largest real cluster ('sold a solution/fix' x4 across user_topics+character_topics) got verdict MERGE, canonical utopic-0fhawqkn, 3 redundant, high confidence — cluster-at-once picked one canonical from four, which pairwise couldn't. Caught + fixed a real bug via the live run: isFactEntry used dedup.looksIncident (/^\[\w+\]/) which MISSES '[scene recap]' (space in tag), so recaps leaked into the fact population; tightened to /^\s*\[/ to match the measurement (curator correctly ruled the leaked recaps distinct, so no harm — but they shouldn't have been there). 7 offline tests (clusterFacts pure + sweepLedger orchestration w/ injected curate); 205 total pass. KNOWN LIMITATION (filed): lexical clustering misses the Warlock pair (semantically-identical, lexically-divergent) — embedding upgrade is the follow-up.

title: Bound the live curator's ledger view (search_entries) for mature ledgers
description: The curator's search_entries returns ALL active entries in the scope. Fine for the small/temp ledgers it's been proven on, but professor_mari has ~4250 entries — dumping that into the curator's context is expensive AND triages (the Ledger Pattern failure), which would make live shadow runs slow and the audit verdicts (hence the log) soupy. The live queue task already carries the KNOWN colliding entry id (againstId), so the fix: seed runCurator with that focus entry + return a BOUNDED, relevance-ranked slice from search_entries (the colliding entry always included), not the whole ledger. Needed before b4n shadow runs are trustworthy on real (mature) ledgers. Relates to b4n, egp (Ledger Pattern context-budget windows).
close_reason: Shipped + verified on the real 4685-entry professor_mari ledger. selectLedgerView pins the structurally-flagged colliding entry, ranks the rest by jaccardSimilarity to the candidate (the same lexical measure that flagged the collision), caps at 50, reports 'showing K of M'. runCurator gains focusId (pinned + called out in the kickoff prompt); the drain passes the task's againstId. Live shadow proof: view = 50 of 4685 (focus pinned); shadow drain reconciled in 15.3s -> DUPLICATE, confidence high, and ranking surfaced the CANONICAL warlock entry (ctopic-bbupy8fl) as the target even though a different warlock entry was the seeded focus — the bound didn't hide the relevant fact. Lexical ranking now; embedding-cosine ranking is a clean future upgrade if lexically-distant-but-semantically-close entries are missed. 4 offline selectLedgerView tests; 198 total pass. Unblocks running b4n shadow on mature ledgers; mjp (confidence-gated apply) is the next phase.

title: Multi-pass judge consensus for durability (single-pass judge is non-deterministic on the hardest borderline)
description: The durability judge (judgeDurableFacts) is a single LLM pass and is NON-DETERMINISTIC on the hardest borderline case: a transient scene event wearing a leading identity clause ('Mari is a professor who recently engaged in sex on her desk...'). Verified live during 967: that exact sentence dropped in one run and was KEPT in the next, even though it is a worked DROP example in the tightened JUDGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT. Prompt-fiddling cannot close this — it is single-pass variance. FIX (same medicine as the extraction consensus shipped in 967): run the judge N passes and keep a candidate only if it survives a MAJORITY (conservative posture: drop unless kept in >= ceil(N/2) passes). Borderline facts that flip get dropped; robust durable facts that survive every pass stay. Cost is N judge calls (one extra batched call each), bounded. Gated/opt-in like FACTS_PASSES so the default path is unchanged. Calibration fixtures live in src/__tests__/judge-calibration.test.ts. NOTE — distinct from this: the SYMMETRIC framing gap (durable lore/identity framed as transient — 'Mari recognizes the system... in this moment', 'in this moment, is the chair') is a PROMPT-level problem, NOT fixed by consensus (the judge drops it CONSISTENTLY across passes). Documented as a fixture + comment in ambient.ts/judge-calibration.test.ts for whoever revisits the prompt; file separately if pursued. Relates to 967, 1dn, egp (Ledger Pattern).
notes: Variance evidence from 967 (live, claude-opus-4-6, repeated runs of the SAME inputs): CONCRETE durables (a named event 'discovered the fraud engine', a named dynamic 'chaos goblin') were kept in 100% of runs. ABSTRACT durables ('strong CS background, values predictability/observability') flipped — dropped 2/3 runs, kept 1/3. The targeted transient ('wants to ruin the chair during intimacy') dropped ~4/5 but survived once. The desk-sex 'professor who recently had sex' transient flipped the other way (dropped once, kept once). Net: the judge is reliably NOISY on borderline (both directions), stable only on concrete facts. Consensus over N passes with a conservative keep-threshold (drop unless kept in >= ceil(N/2)) is the fix; it stabilizes the abstract-durable recall AND the masked-transient precision at once. Fixtures + the probe harness are in src/__tests__/judge-calibration.test.ts (run ME_LIVE_JUDGE=1).
close_reason: Implemented + verified live. judgeDurableFacts now runs N independent passes (MARINARA_EXTENDER_JUDGE_PASSES, default 1 = behaviour+cost unchanged) and keeps on a strict majority of SUCCESSFUL passes (floor(ok/2)+1); a dead pass abstains, all-fail = fail-open keep-all. LIVE PROOF at passes=3 over the exact set that flipped single-pass: across 3 consensus-runs, 5/5 ideal EVERY run — the abstract durable that dropped 2/3 single-pass is now reliably KEPT (recall recovered), the masked-transient (desk-sex) + episodic-desire that flipped are now reliably DROPPED (precision recovered). Both failure directions fixed at once = 'more passes = higher confidence.' Majority not unanimous is deliberate: over-drops are silent data loss, over-keeps are caught by the apply-review ledger. 7 offline consensus tests (injected judgePass); --judge-passes flag added to backfill-scene-facts.mjs. The symmetric framing gap (durable content framed as transient) remains a separate prompt-level concern, documented in ambient.ts + judge-calibration.test.ts.

title: Agentic fact-reconciliation curator (Claude Agent SDK, CLI-session auth)
description: Implements FR3 (MarinaraExtender-15x) as a tool-using Agent SDK loop instead of a single gated prompt. A curator agent adjudicates a candidate fact against existing same-subject ledger entries and emits one of the FR3 decisions: UPDATE (supersede), EXPAND (both true, keep both), DISTINCT (false collision, keep both), NEGATE (incoming disproves existing -> supersede as negative fact). Built on @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk with IN-PROCESS MCP tools wrapping the storage layer: search_entries + read_entry (read), and act tools that map to saveFact + supersedeEntry (FR2/6x9, already built). Auth: SDK default CLI-session credential resolution (logged-in claude CLI / Claude Code) — no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, no baseURL. Feature is ADDITIVE + opt-in: gates on (a) an env flag and (b) the CLI being logged in; the provider-agnostic OpenAI-compat core (Ollama/linkapi) is untouched. Ledger discipline like backfill-scene-facts: dry-run records the curator's decisions to a per-subject ledger file (act tools are no-ops in dry mode), --apply executes EXACTLY those decisions (no re-run) — essential because an agent loop is even less deterministic than single-pass extraction. Offline/on-demand script first (scripts/reconcile-facts.mjs), not the live turn path; live FR1-gated integration is a follow-up. Decision vocab + supersede-as-tier-move reused verbatim from 2r3/15x/6x9.
notes: Offline curator COMPLETE + pushed (commit 25beee7): src/reconcile.ts + scripts/reconcile-facts.mjs + dedup.createEntry refactor + 6 offline applyDecision tests (177 total pass), typecheck + build clean. NOT yet verified: the live runCurator agent loop (needs a logged-in claude CLI session + real model calls) — pending TC's end-to-end run via reconcile-facts.mjs. Live FR1-gated turn-path integration split out to MarinaraExtender-b4n.
close_reason: Verified end-to-end on real data (Mari scenes, Agent SDK on CLI-session auth). DUPLICATE/EXPAND/CREATE/DISTINCT all exercised. Decisive proof: on 'Unpacking Hargrove' the curator caught a bioinformatics-vs-computational-linguist identity conflict (a scenario-frame extraction artifact) and chose DISTINCT — explicitly REFUSING to overwrite established canon. Judgment, not rule-execution. UPDATE/NEGATE didn't fire live (no retcon candidate in these scenes); the verdict->action mapping is unit-tested (applyDecision, 6 tests). Live FR1-gated turn-path integration continues in b4n.

title: Apply the Ledger Pattern: size LLM windows by the active model's context, not a fixed chunk count
description: Formalize and apply the Ledger Pattern (docs/ledger-pattern.md, bd remembered). The fact pass (ingestSceneFacts) currently uses a fixed SCENE_FACTS_BATCH=5 chunks regardless of model - that throttles a 200k-context remote model to the same window an 8k local model needs, and is model-blind. Apply the allocation rule: size each slice to ~1/6 of the ACTIVE model's context window (token-estimated), so the window scales with the model. Needs: (1) a per-model context-window value (config/lookup; default conservative, e.g. 8k for unknown local, larger for known remote models); (2) token-budgeted windowing in ingestSceneFacts (and reusable for digest/recap/arc-render); (3) optional on-disk ledger for the fact pass so a long backfill is resumable + each slice's extraction is inspectable before assembly. Generalize into a small shared helper (processInLedger: input, chunker, perSliceFn, assembleFn, contextBudget) so import beats, fact extraction, digest, scene recap, and arc rendering share one disciplined implementation. Relates to 1dn (surfaced it), nqy (per-model config surface), tcx (budget config).

title: Easy-configure OpenAI-compatible model endpoint (UI + clearer naming + bad-key diagnostic)
description: TC: 'set up an OpenAI-compatible frontend people can easily configure.' The capability already EXISTS via env (callExternal hits {DIGEST_UPSTREAM}/v1/chat/completions with DIGEST_MODEL + API_KEY - works against OpenAI, OpenRouter, linkapi.ai, any compatible server; TC already runs claude-opus-4-6 via linkapi.ai this way). Gaps: (1) env-only, no UI - surface upstream/model/key (and the new MARINARA_EXTENDER_FACTS_MODEL routing) in the setup page / panel, reusing the existing /api/save-key pattern; (2) naming - the MARINARA_EXTENDER_DIGEST_* vars are legacy/confusing for what is now the general external-model config; add clearer aliases (e.g. _API_URL / _API_MODEL) keeping the old names working; (3) BAD-KEY DIAGNOSTIC - found live: linkapi.ai returns 401 Invalid token and callExternal silently swallows it (returns null -> falls back to local), so a dead key is invisible. Add a probe like embeddingsStatus() that reports auth ok / 401 / endpoint-down, surfaced in the banner + /api/health, so 'why is quality bad / why did facts not capture' is diagnosable. Relates to tcx (budget UI - same settings surface) and 1dn (facts need the strong model). NOTE: routing facts to the engine's own chat model is NOT feasible - the sidecar has no access to the engine's API connection/credentials; the configurable external endpoint is the answer.
notes: Live datapoint reinforcing this: the 401 that blocked the 1dn demo was NOT an expired key - linkapi.ai did a TOS change that invalidated the token. Third-party reseller endpoints (cheap frontier-model proxies) churn their terms/keys, and our failure mode is silent (callExternal swallows the 401, degrades to local, no signal). So provider-switch ease + the bad-key/auth-status diagnostic are load-bearing, not polish: a user must be able to (a) see that their endpoint is rejecting them, and (b) repoint to a different OpenAI-compatible provider quickly. Default config should probably steer toward first-party providers (OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter) over resellers for stability.

title: Mobile / remote sidecar access - extension + server are hardcoded localhost-only
description:
Tester report (aubrii, Android): the 'memory server not running' banner shows on every load on mobile, no matter how many reloads/restarts. Root cause is architectural, not a bug: the extender is same-machine-only by construction. (1) marinara-extender.js hardcodes MEMORY_EXTENDER='http://127.0.0.1:3001' - on a phone that's the phone's own localhost. (2) index.ts binds app.listen host '127.0.0.1' (loopback only) - refuses any off-box connection by design. So a phone browser talking to a desktop-hosted sidecar can't reach it on either count. Base Marinara RP still works (engine-only), which is why it 'feels like it works' while memory is silently disconnected.

To support mobile/remote (browser on a different device than the sidecar):
1. Extension: make the sidecar base URL configurable (setting / localStorage / served-in config) instead of the 127.0.0.1 constant, so it can point at the host's Tailscale or LAN IP.
2. Server: env-controlled bind host (e.g. MARINARA_EXTENDER_BIND_HOST, default 127.0.0.1) so advanced users can bind a reachable interface; keep loopback as the safe default.
3. CORS: allow the phone's origin (MARINARA_EXTENDER_ALLOWED_ORIGIN already exists - wire it through).
4. Also fix the offline banner copy for this case: 'Start it (Extender_start.bat), then reload' is wrong/unhelpful on a phone - detect a non-localhost/unreachable case and explain.

SECURITY: loopback bind is a deliberate boundary - the sidecar holds private memory and can proxy LLM calls. Exposing it must be gated (Tailscale/VPN strongly recommended, or an auth token), never an open-LAN default. Document this loudly. Relates to szf (install story). Decide scope before promising mobile support to testers.
notes:
CLIENT-SIDE UNLOCK DONE (34c8e61): single configurable sidecar address. Loader sets window.__meSidecar=SIDECAR; extension reads it with 127.0.0.1 fallback. One edit point (the loader's SIDECAR line), both call sites follow, default preserved. Verified: unset->localhost, set->tailnet IP; compiled loader emits the global.

REMAINING for end-to-end case B (server side, NOT done): the sidecar still binds 127.0.0.1 (index.ts), so it is only reachable at a tailnet/LAN IP if something exposes it - a non-loopback bind (proposed: MARINARA_EXTENDER_BIND_HOST env, default 127.0.0.1) and/or CORS for the engine origin (MARINARA_EXTENDER_ALLOWED_ORIGIN, exists), OR a Tailscale serve / port-forward that proxies to loopback. TC's phone test will reveal which: if pointing the loader's SIDECAR at the PC tailnet IP:3001 just works, his Tailscale already proxies loopback and case B = client change (done) + docs. If it can't connect, add the bind-host env (small, default-safe) and re-test. Security gate still applies to any non-loopback bind (Tailscale/VPN/auth, never open-LAN default).

title: One-click updater requires a git checkout - ZIP/release installs can't update
description: Surfaced by a tester's 'fatal: not a git repository' report (immediate stderr leaks fixed in b14f84f). Deeper gap: the one-click updater (update-sidecar.ps1) updates via 'git pull --ff-only', so any install without .git - GitHub 'Download ZIP', a release tarball, or a manual file copy - fundamentally cannot use it. The updater now detects this and explains the manual path instead of failing destructively, but those users still have NO in-product update route. Also, the fix for any non-git bug can't reach these users THROUGH the updater (chicken-and-egg) - they must re-clone or manually copy. Options for v1.2+: (a) document git clone as the supported/recommended install so auto-update works (cheapest); (b) add a non-git update path - download the latest release asset, extract, replace code while preserving data/ and .env (real feature, the robust answer); (c) ship as a packaged installer. Relates to szf (install pipeline) and e87. Decide install story before widening the tester pool.

title: User-configurable token budget (UI for the per-scope budgets)
description:
GOAL: let users set how many tokens the extender spends on injected memory. Interim measure - keep the footprint small now, optimize token usage properly later; for now just expose the dials.

SCOPE: SMALL. The backend already exists. loader.ts getBudgets() reads three env vars at call time (no restart needed): MARINARA_EXTENDER_BUDGET_CHAT (4000), MARINARA_EXTENDER_BUDGET_CHARACTER (2000), MARINARA_EXTENDER_BUDGET_GLOBAL (1000). Eidetic mode already bypasses budgeting entirely. So this feature is purely a UX wrapper over an existing mechanism, NOT new core logic.

WORK:
1. Settings UI - three number inputs (chat / character / global) in the ledger panel or setup page, with the current effective values prefilled and a live 'total ~N tokens/turn' readout.
2. Persistence - write the three values to sidecar/.env (reuse the /api/save-key pattern that already writes .env) and update process.env in-memory so they take effect immediately without a restart. New small endpoint e.g. POST /api/budgets.
3. ONE gotcha to enforce: the sum of scope budgets must stay under the engine lorebook tokenBudget (currently 16384, set by ensureLorebook) or the engine silently drops over-budget entries - the exact bug fixed for the default case. So either cap the inputs so chat+character+global <= lorebook budget, or raise the lorebook budget to match the chosen total. Surface this as a guardrail, not a footgun.

ACCEPTANCE: a user sets character budget to e.g. 1000 in the panel, the next turn's injected block honors it (visible in the [L] log 'char:X/1000 tokens'), no restart, and the UI prevents a total that would exceed the lorebook budget. Relates to 20z (per-character/per-model budget overrides - this is the simpler global version).

title: Consolidate launcher proliferation - 5+ start scripts invite port collisions
description: Repo root ships Extender_start.bat, Marinara_Extender_Start.bat, start-memory-extender.bat, start.ps1 (plus stop/update). Users launch via different ones at different times and double-launch, racing for port 3001 - the root of the keeps-closing report (fixed defensively in 8131f7c with port guards in start.ps1 Start-Sidecar and index.ts EADDRINUSE handling). Follow-up: pick ONE canonical launcher (start.ps1 via Extender_start.bat is the polished one with the restart console), make the others either forward to it or be removed, and document the single entry point in the README so a fresh install has exactly one way to start.
close_reason: Consolidated to one launcher per TC's choice. TC uses Extender_start.bat (the polished start.ps1 with the [R] restart console + new port guard). Deleted the redundant May-14 pair (start-memory-extender.bat, stop-memory-extender.bat) and the June-11 Marinara_Extender_Start.bat I had added (plus its run-sidecar.ps1 worker). Repointed the updater relaunch and all user-facing 'not running' messages from Marinara_Extender_Start.bat to Extender_start.bat. Root left with: Extender_start.bat (start), Marinara_Extender_Update.bat (update), start.ps1.

title: Tier-3 roster scoping: Aurora (Narrator alias) collects in-scene junk facts from scenes she is not in; persona names should map to user
description: Observed 2026-06-12 in Scene: Jurisprudence, Soft Launch (Mari/Priya/Thomas — no Aurora): 6 of 8 ambient facts routed to character:aurora with summaries like 'Character mentions a list being mentioned' and 'User presses their mouth to character's shoulder' — in-scene actions, not facts about Aurora. Two stacked causes: (1) the subject roster is GLOBAL (all known identities), so the model assigns narration-voice trivia to Aurora because her 'Narrator' alias makes her the only narrator-shaped roster name — in scenes she is not even in; (2) the 8B local classifier ignores the prompt's 'skip pure actions / in-scene roleplay events' rule. ALSO: subject='Thomas Collier' (TC's persona) was demoted to chat scope — guard worked, no pollution, but persona names should resolve to 'user' (session ledger). FIXES: (a) extension sends the chat's actual participant characterIds/names on process-turn; sidecar restricts the subject roster to participants + user — Aurora drops out of scenes she is not in (also tightens tier-2); (b) persona-name → user mapping (extension knows the active persona name; send it, treat as user synonym in routing); (c) consider a junk-fact gate: drop tier-3 facts whose summary matches pure-action shapes (Character/User + body-part/motion verbs) or add one-shot GOOD/BAD examples like the thread-label fix. CLEANUP: ~6 junk entries dated 2026-06-12 sit in aurora/char-topics pending TC approval to delete.
notes: Post-close addendum (TC decision): ctopic-gfmttbuj was real character data misrouted, not junk — moved (not deleted) from aurora to professor_mari per TC, content untouched, created date preserved. Underlying beat moved too: aurora/beat-97b2b4c0e13d -> professor_mari/beat-f66a5a6c8306 with speaker corrected to Professor Mari. Verified: present under mari, gone from aurora.
close_reason: All three fixes shipped in 8fd7fc5, live on sidecar. (a) Extension sends chat characterIds + active persona name on process-turn; buildSubjectRoster restricts the analyzer roster to scene participants (global fallback for old clients) — Aurora drops out of scenes she is not in. (b) matchesSessionName(subject, personaName) treats player-persona subjects as user in both tiers — persona facts land in the session ledger. (c) Tier-3 SKIP one-shot examples (in-scene actions are not facts). Cleanup: 8 junk entries removed from aurora by exact id; 2 genuine Aurora dev-chat entries kept; ctopic-gfmttbuj (Professor Mari subject filed under aurora, pre-fix misroute via Narrator alias) left in place for TC review. 130/130 tests. VERIFY LIVE: after a Marinara page reload, scene turns should show roster-restricted subjects; Aurora gets no new entries from scenes without her.

title: pln follow-up: thread registry health check — cast-list label detector, fragmentation metrics, scene-title hint
description: First live threads showed the cast-list failure mode: in a chat literally named 'Scene: Jurisprudence, Soft Launch' the model labeled the thread 'professor_mari_and_priya'. Immediate mitigations shipped (88f2204 + follow-on): characterName now sent on process-turn, one-shot GOOD/BAD examples in the thread prompt rule ('Porsche test drive' / 'jurisprudence soft launch' good; 'thomas_and_mari' / 'professor_mari_and_priya' bad — the cast is not the story), underscore sanitation, bad label regenerated by hand. THIS ISSUE = the durable guardrails: (1) registry health check — a lint pass (cleanup-style, or piggyback on promotion) that flags labels whose significant tokens are a subset of the thread's participants' names (cast-list detector), plus fragmentation metrics (threads-per-chat, single-beat thread ratio) so prompt regressions surface in numbers not vibes; (2) consider passing the CHAT NAME as a prompt hint — Marinara scene chats are usually named for the arc ('Scene: Jurisprudence, Soft Launch'), which is exactly the right first-thread label; extension would send chatName on process-turn; (3) a relabel API/UI affordance so bad labels can be fixed without hand-editing registry.yaml. Parent: MarinaraExtender-pln.
close_reason: Shipped: looksCastList lint (mint-time console warning + health endpoint), threadRegistryHealth metrics (totals, threads-per-chat, single-beat ratio, suspects), GET /api/threads + PATCH relabel + POST close routes (CSRF-guarded), scene-title hint from the extension riding the analyzer prompt as preferred new-thread label. Verified live on the running sidecar. Requires a Marinara page reload to start sending sceneTitle (morning). 140/140 tests.

title: Story-import path lacks subject routing — filter-not-route writes all beats under the import target (RP transcripts misattribute)
description: runSentimentPipeline filters passing chunks by speakerMatches against the import target / characters list and writes EVERYTHING under the single import characterId (pipeline.ts ~107-115). RP transcripts are mostly narration (no 'Name:' prefixes) so the chunker labels them Narrator; povCharacter reassigns ALL narration to one character. Net effect: importing a multi-character RP scene cannot put a co-star's beats in their own ledger — confirmed by TC's first re-import of Scene: Test Drive Transgression (Priya got nothing). The 6qx subject-attribution fix (analyzer roster + per-beat routing) landed on the LIVE path only; port the same mechanism to the import pipeline: attribute per-beat subject during analysis and route to resolveNameToKey/holding-pool instead of filtering by import target. title: CSRF protection on mutating sidecar requests (deferred from v1.0)
description: Deferred from v1.0, still owed. The sidecar is localhost-bound but our extension runs inside the Marinara origin over plain HTTP — any page in the same browser can fire cross-origin POSTs at 127.0.0.1:3001, and mutating endpoints currently accept them. Add CSRF defense on all mutating routes: simplest robust option for a localhost sidecar is a static token minted at startup, handed to the extension via the loader bridge, required as a header on every non-GET; same-origin checks via Origin header as belt-and-braces. Lower urgency than the data-loss bugs (attack requires a hostile page in the user's browser) but real exposure.
close_reason: Shipped in 99f649b, live on sidecar. csrf.ts: per-process token; every non-GET /api/* with a browser Origin must pass the CORS allowlist AND carry x-me-csrf (token served at GET /api/csrf-token, CORS-gated read). Origin-less requests (node tooling, tests) pass — browsers always send Origin on POST so attackers cannot omit it. Closes the real pre-existing hole: cross-origin simple requests executed server-side on body-less mutating endpoints despite unreadable responses. Extension memFetch fetches/caches/refreshes the token (403 = sidecar restart = retry once with fresh token); raw analyze-beats NDJSON fetch carries it explicitly (no retry — user re-clicks on stale). 6 integration tests on real routes; 127/127. KNOWN trust-model limit: any loopback-served page is still an allowed origin per cors.ts and can read the token — unchanged from v1.0; tightening would need MARINARA_EXTENDER_ALLOWED_ORIGIN pinning.

title: Trait-vs-incident dedup: emotionally loaded incident beats collapse into personality entries — needs distinguishing signal at dedup layer
description: Emotionally loaded INCIDENT beats (specific events) keep getting collapsed into TRAIT/personality entries by dedup — the event is eaten because it structurally resembles the standing trait. Needs a distinguishing signal at the dedup layer (dedup.ts): incident-vs-trait is a different axis than the FR1 fact-correction case. IMPORTANT INTERPLAY: FR1 (MarinaraExtender-4eu) explicitly states 'character_topics keeps accumulate-dedup as-is' — this issue AMENDS that assumption, so sequence with/after FR1 or fold both decision changes into one dedup.ts revision to avoid editing the same decision logic twice. Diagnose jointly with the porsche attribution bug (same fingerprint surface).
close_reason: Shipped in 1401be4 (live on sidecar). character_topics entries carry kind (incident|trait); incidents never collapse into traits at the 0.35 band (the arc accumulates), incident-vs-incident dedups at 0.6 (only same-moment recaptures), legacy entries classify by the [emotion] prefix. All five beat-companion call sites tagged incident; tier-3 ambient character facts tagged trait. Cross-character half of the collapse was already fixed by 6qx subject routing. 12 matrix tests, 121/121 suite.

title: kNN packaging: ship embedding candidate-generator default-ON with env kill switch
description: Product/packaging decision, separate scope from whether the ceiling works — must NOT gate the ceiling. DECISION (made): default-ON / opt-OUT, not opt-in. Rationale: opt-in causes SILENT DEGRADATION — the median user gets entity-anchored (restricted) arc clustering that looks fine because they have nothing to compare to, and you cannot later diagnose 'recap does not catch X' because half the user base runs a different feature set than the docs describe. 274MB is one more ollama pull (user already accepted a model download in step 1; same mental category) and the runtime cost is a different endpoint on an already-running ollama, not a new service. Posture mirrors the time-sense feature: default-on with a kill switch beats default-off with a 'did you know' footnote. WHAT: enable MARINARA_EXTENDER_EMBED_MODEL=nomic-embed-text by default; add 'ollama pull nomic-embed-text' (~274MB) to start.ps1; provide an env var to DISABLE kNN for lean installs. COUPLING: this is what makes hardening H2 (cross-entity/thematic arc recall) fire for the median user. Parent: MarinaraExtender-cz3.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh install pulls nomic-embed-text and runs kNN by default; a documented env var disables it; the ceiling tier functions in both modes (degraded recall when disabled).
close_reason: Shipped in 77fd63a as embeddings.ts: DEFAULT-ON (nomic-embed-text) with MARINARA_EXTENDER_EMBED_MODEL=0/off kill switch per the default-on posture; chunker refactored onto the shared module; exercised live by the arc-promotion kNN generator on both characters; .env.example updated.

Remaining: user pastes the loader once in a real browser to confirm fetch+new Function execution works under Marinara's CSP (Marinara already runs extensions via new Function, so expected to pass).
close_reason: VERIFIED WORKING in-browser (user, 2026-06-04). CSP-safe blob loader: pasted/uploaded once, fetches the live extension from the sidecar each Marinara load and runs it via a blob <script> (Marinara CSP forbids eval/new Function — first attempt failed with EvalError, fixed). title: FR1: lane-aware dedup — stop eating fact corrections
description: The 0.35 Jaccard dedup drops 'user's sister is Lin' as a duplicate of 'user's sister is Mei' — the meaning-carrying token is exactly what Jaccard ignores. Make dedup lane-aware in dedup.ts createEntryIfUnique/isDuplicate: character_topics keeps accumulate-dedup as-is; user_topics changes the DECISION — high structural overlap + small symmetric difference is the signature of a CORRECTION, so route it to a supersession-candidate (flag/return) instead of silently dropping. Interim (before FR2 lands): err toward KEEPING over dropping for user_topics. This step's output (the candidate collisions) is what FR3's LLM pass consumes.
close_reason: FR1 shipped in 1401be4 (live on sidecar). user_topics similarity hits with the correction signature (jaccard>=0.5, symmetric diff of 1-4 content-bearing words, function-word stoplist) are CREATED instead of silently dropped, and the collision pair is recorded to data/supersession-candidates.yaml — the input queue FR2 (MarinaraExtender-6x9, now unblocked) and FR3 consume, exposed via readSupersessionCandidates(). Plain restatements still dedup. Tests cover Mei/Lin correction, restatement, and unrelated-fact cases.

title: FR2: lane-aware supersession for facts (active/superseded, tier move not delete)
description: Add supersession to user_topics. A newer fact marks the older one status=superseded with a supersedes/supersededBy pointer (the link field lands HERE, not in FR4). Superseded entries drop out of the Current/active set (loader selection) but stay in the pool — kept-but-not-current — so negative facts remain queryable. Wire into the FR1 candidate path: a flagged same-subject collision supersedes rather than duplicates. Depends on FR1.
close_reason: Shipped: supersedeEntry (pointer + tier move to cold, never delete) wired into the FR1 correction branch; old fact drops out of Current (hot filter + cold-recall skip — rehydration cannot resurrect a stale fact next to its replacement), stays queryable in cold at full fidelity. Candidates file carries applied flag; GET /api/supersessions serves the audit trail. DEVIATION from spec text: used a separate supersededBy field instead of status=superseded — per TC's own EntryStatus audit corollary (cross-boundary-type-change memory): never widen a serialized enum; the two type systems meet at exactly two deliberate consumers (selectEntries, coldRecall). FR3 (15x) now has its input queue with applied-state; FR4 (3pl) has the pointer + cold retention it needs for rollback.

title: Fact reconciliation: feelings accumulate, facts supersede
description: Cross-chat memory currently UNIFIES (one identityKey pool) and DEDUPES (Jaccard 0.35) but does NOT reconcile. Principle: feelings accumulate (character_topics beats — the arc is the point), facts supersede (user_topics — later replaces earlier). A superseded fact is a tier move, NOT a delete: kept-but-not-current, queryable as a negative fact. Layered roadmap (NOT a single fix), ordered so we stop eating inputs before building on top: lane-aware dedup -> lane-aware supersession -> LLM reconciliation (gated by the structural detector) -> rollback/audit UI. See bd memory fact-vs-feeling-memory-principle. Supersedes the earlier cross-chat-reconciliation note.

title: Honest retrieval signal: stamp lastRetrievedAt on use, not on load
description: Loader stamped lastRetrievedAt on every surfaced entry — conflating 'loaded into context' with 'actually used', which inflated the recency signal and the feedback loop (loaded -> looks retrieved -> stays). Fixed: loader stamps lastAccessed + retrievalCount only; recordRecitation now stamps lastRetrievedAt (+ lastAccessed) when the model demonstrably uses the entry (recitation). daysSinceRetrieval (demotion) and the Current-cache recency now reflect real use. Safe for retention: prune guard (retrievalCount>0) and core permanence unchanged; never-used entries fall back to lastAccessed so imports aren't pruned.

title: Soft-clock: weight explicit presence statements over session time; stop break-nagging

MEMORY_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS now tell the character to treat presence as fact and NEVER tell the user to rest/take a break based on session length. updateSoftClock takes the user's message (process-turn passes userMessageText).

title: Strip lone UTF-16 surrogates from entries (invalid-high-surrogate)

Added stripLoneSurrogates() applied in writeEntry (summary+content) and upsertIndexEntry (summary), the universal storage choke points. New entries are clean; existing ones get cleaned on re-import.

title: Memory retrieval observability — confirm characters are actually using memories
description: Need a clearer, ideally user-facing way to see whether a character retrieved/used memories on a turn, rather than inferring from sidecar logs. Existing signals today: (1) sidecar logs '[ME] memory loaded — key:.. chat:..' per memory-block write; (2) ME_DEBUG=1 -> [ME:loader] logs 'entries selected — chat:X char:Y global:Z' and the selected entry ids each turn; (3) recitation detection logs when a surfaced memory was echoed in the response and bumps recitationCount; (4) retrievalCount/recitationCount/lastRetrievedAt live on each index entry. Possible work: surface per-turn 'surfaced N memories (which ones)' in the ledger UI; show retrieval/recitation counts + lastRetrieved per entry in the panel; a small 'last turn' debug overlay. Deferred by user for later examination.
close_reason: Shipped (client-only, e8-series commit): panel activity line — '🧠 last turn: N memories in context · +X saved' with click-to-expand surfaced summaries per scope, refreshing live each processed turn; per-entry ⟲retrievalCount ✓recitationCount badges with lastRetrievedAt on hover. Data was already flowing (SurfacedEntry in process-turn response, counts on index rows) — this is pure presentation. The deeper signals (recitation detection, retrievalCount mechanics) were already in place and unchanged. Needs Marinara page reload to appear; flagged for TC's morning verification.

title: Character-names field in import, remembered per character
description: Import panel has a Names field (comma-separated) sent as the characters[] speaker filter, so 'Mari' pins 'Dr. Mari Zielińska'. Persisted in localStorage per characterId and auto-prefilled on character switch — type it once, remembered after.
close_reason: Implemented together: tabbed ledger + scroll preservation + cancel + external/bigger-windows + remembered names. node --check ok, tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Cancel/restart a running story import from the ledger
description: Import was one un-abortable await. Added Cancel button (AbortController), client aborts the fetch; server detects req close -> AbortController -> pipeline/parser check signal at window/chunk boundaries and stop before writing beats. Client resets immediately so a restart is possible.
close_reason: Implemented together: tabbed ledger + scroll preservation + cancel + external/bigger-windows + remembered names. node --check ok, tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: External-API attribution checkbox + bigger windows
description: Import panel checkbox routes attribution straight to external API and uses larger windows (28k vs 12k chars), fewer round-trips and more consistent speaker labels. Server: ParseStoryOptions.useExternal; attributeWindow skips local; external max_tokens raised to 16384.
close_reason: Implemented together: tabbed ledger + scroll preservation + cancel + external/bigger-windows + remembered names. node --check ok, tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Attribution phase shows no progress during multi-window story import
description: parseStoryToMessages attributes long inputs in N sequential LLM calls (up to 120s each on local) but emitted no progress after 'attributing in N windows' — looked hung. Added onWindow callback; ingest-story ticks a per-window bar (Progress.tick unit='window'). Server-side only.
close_reason: onWindow callback + per-window bar in ingest-story; tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Story-import progress reporting in sidecar console (toggleable, default on)
description: Live progress for story imports and chat-history analysis in the sidecar console: 'importing X — chunk i/N [bar] pct%', stage lines (attributing / parsing complete, analyzing sentiment), per-chunk ERROR with specific reason, and a done summary. New progress.ts (Progress class). analyzeChunks takes an onItem callback; pipeline threads ticks+errors. ingest-story accepts title (file name) and progress override; extension sends the file name. Toggle MARINARA_EXTENDER_PROGRESS (default on; 0 to silence), shown at startup.
close_reason: progress.ts + analyzer onItem + pipeline ticks + ingest-story title/progress + extension sends file name + env toggle (default on). Output verified; tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Drop Marinara embeddings sidecar; route chunk embeddings through Ollama
description: The only remaining Marinara Engine sidecar dependency was the chunker's embeddings call (MARINARA_ENGINE_URL/api/sidecar/v1/embeddings) for semantic merging. Replaced with Ollama's /v1/embeddings, opt-in via MARINARA_EXTENDER_EMBED_MODEL; unset = speaker-turn grouping (no external sidecar, no warning spam). All other model calls already used Ollama; corrected stale 'Gemma/Marinara sidecar' comments and error messages in digest/analyzer/pipeline/classifier. .env: removed unused MARINARA_ENGINE_URL, documented opt-in embed model.
close_reason: chunker uses Ollama /v1/embeddings (opt-in EMBED_MODEL); stale comments/errors fixed; tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Pre-attribution false-positives on prose with stray colons
description: isPreAttributed counted any 'Capitalized: text' line, so date headers (Date:/Chat:), literal Narrator:, and prose sentences with a mid-line colon ('The one that meant ...:') hit the >=3 threshold. Story got treated as a dialogue log, skipped the LLM attribution pass, and the chunker split on garbage speakers (Chat, Date, The one that meant) -> 0-2 matching beats. Fix: require recurring speakers (count>=2) covering >=3 lines AND attributed lines >=40% of non-empty lines; blacklist header words; lazy speaker-label capture. Regression tests added.
close_reason: isPreAttributed now requires recurring non-blacklisted speakers covering >=3 lines AND >=40% attributed-line fraction; lazy label capture. 4 regression tests, all 53 pass.

title: Local model returns prose instead of JSON (ambient/digest/analyzer)
description: Local Ollama calls ask for JSON in the prompt but don't force it, so small models (dolphin3:8b) return prose and the code falls back to the external API every turn ([ME:ambient] local model returned prose). Fix: send response_format json on local calls. Ambient returns a bare array -> wrap schema as {facts:[...]} so json_object mode is valid; make parser accept both shapes. Apply to digest and analyzer local calls too.
close_reason: response_format json_object forced on local calls (ambient/digest/analyzer). Ambient schema wrapped as {facts:[...]}; parser accepts both shapes. Cuts the prose-fallback.

title: Analyzer context uses filtered neighbors, not true adjacent chunks
description: analyzeChunks sets before/after to passing[i-1]/passing[i+1] (nearest threshold-passing, speaker-filtered chunks), but the documented intent is the actual surrounding conversation. Distant cherry-picked beats are shown as 'preceding/following context for tone', degrading every analysis. Fix: thread the full ordered classification list through and pick true adjacent chunks by position. Same in api.ts Tier 2 caller.
close_reason: analyzeChunks now takes the full ordered classification list and picks true adjacent chunks by index for before/after context. pipeline and Tier 2 both pass the full list.

title: memory-block resolves identity without characterName -> cryptic slug
description: GET /api/memory-block calls resolveIdentity(characterId) with no name. It fires on session load (syncMemoryBlock), before the first process-turn. For a brand-new character this creates the stable identityKey from slugify(cardId) = the cryptic card ID instead of the character's name. process-turn later fixes the display name but the directory/key stays cryptic, defeating the identity system. Fix: have the extension send characterName to memory-block and pass it through to resolveIdentity.
close_reason: memory-block accepts characterName; extension sends it. New characters get readable identity slug.

title: Bracket bookmark weight always defaults to 0.5
description: MEMORY_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS tells the model to emit [bookmark: ... weight=0.8 ...] (unquoted), but writer.ts parseBracketParam only matches quoted values (key="..."). So weight is always undefined -> falls back to 0.5 for every bracket bookmark. surfaceBookmarks rolls Math.random()<weight, so all bookmarks surface at 50% regardless of the model's 0.1-0.9 signal. Fix: make parseBracketParam accept unquoted numeric values (key=value up to comma/bracket) OR quote weight in the instructions. Verified with a parse test.
close_reason: parseBracketParam now accepts unquoted values (weight=0.8) and quoted values with commas. Verified weight parses to 0.8.

title: Recitation detection: increment recitationCount when memory is used
description:
Why: Retrieval (memory surfaced in context) is a weak signal. Recitation (AI actually used the memory in its response) is a strong signal for promotion. Without detecting recitation, the scoring system is half-blind.

What: process-turn response includes surfacedIds (list of entry IDs that were selected for context that turn). Extension checks the AI response text against each surfaced entry summary using Jaccard similarity. If similarity > 0.3, calls PATCH /api/entries/:id/recite to increment recitationCount. Server recalculates score and triggers promotion if threshold crossed.
notes: Implemented end-to-end. Server: loadContext now returns self-contained surfaced[] (id+summary+scope+scopeId) instead of bare surfacedIds; process-turn forwards it. recordRecitation rewritten to use new serialized mutateIndex() helper (was doing an unlocked read-modify-write that could clobber retrieval-count stamping = corrupt-YAML race). Extension: detectRecitations no longer depends on panelState (covered only chat scope before) — now works for all scopes from the self-contained payload, and compares each response against the PREVIOUS turn's surfaced set (cached in lastSurfaced[chatId]) to avoid a fresh entry self-matching the response it was extracted from. Metric changed from symmetric Jaccard (mathematically ~0 for short summary vs long response, never fired) to asymmetric stopword-filtered containment; verified: clear use=0.50, unrelated=0.00, partial=0.17, stopword-only=0.00 at threshold 0.30. PENDING: live browser verification after extension reinstall + sidecar restart.
close_reason: Implemented; server type-checks, extension syntax-checks, containment metric unit-verified. Live browser test pending reinstall+restart.

title: Tier 3: ambient detail classifier
description:
Why: Throwaway lines that reveal who someone IS get lost because they carry no emotional weight. 'Priya has a level 400 character', 'I grew up in Texas', 'I cried at the end of MGS3' — these make someone feel known rather than just remembered. This is where the magic lives.

What: After each turn, extract candidate sentences from both user and AI messages. Filter to: sentences under 120 chars, containing first-person markers (I/my/me/we/my), not ending in ?. For each candidate, run a fast binary LLM call: 'does this reveal a stable fact about identity, history, preferences, or relationships?' If yes, extract the fact and save as user_topics (user message) or character_topics (AI message). Fires async — does not block lorebook update.
notes: Small local model (dolphin3:8b) handles this well. Binary output keeps it fast. Candidate filtering is the key — reduces LLM calls dramatically. User messages → user_topics, AI messages → character_topics.
close_reason: Closed

title: Tier 2: sentiment capture on live chat turns
description:
Why: Emotional peaks in conversation (breakthroughs, fights, confessions, crying together) should be captured automatically without the AI needing to self-tag. The sentiment pipeline already works for story ingests — it needs to run on live turns too.

What: process-turn receives recent messages (last 20) from extension. Runs classifyChunks on them (CPU only, no LLM). If any chunk exceeds 0.65 salience threshold, fires analyzeChunk async (does not block lorebook update). Saves EmotionalBeat under character beats directory plus a companion character_topics ledger entry describing the moment. Works in both RP and conversation mode.
notes: Threshold starts at 0.65, tune from there. Classifier is pure keyword/regex so it adds no latency. LLM analyzer fires only when threshold crossed. Game mode excluded for now.
close_reason: Closed

title: Tier 1: time-based session snapshots (30 min)
description:
Why: Long collaborative sessions leave no memory trace when nothing trips an importance signal. A 2.5 hour paper editing session is meaningful context that the current self-tagging model misses entirely.

What: Extension tracks lastSnapshotTime per chatId in localStorage. On each new message, if 30+ minutes have elapsed, sends last 40 messages to new /api/snapshot endpoint. Server runs a single lightweight LLM call: summarize what was discussed, extract user facts and ongoing work, save as character-scope entries. Prompt is distinct from the chat-import digest — framed as ongoing session summary not historical review.
notes: Reuses existing digest infrastructure. 30 min is user-specified. Last 40 messages is a reasonable window — tune if needed.
close_reason: Closed

title: Three-tier ambient memory capture system
description:
Why: The current self-tagging model only captures what the AI explicitly decides to flag — it misses two entire categories of valuable memory. A 2.5-hour paper editing session left no trace because nothing tripped an importance signal. Low-signal throwaway details ('Priya's level 400 character', 'the MGS3 tears') are what make someone feel *known* rather than just remembered, and no current signal catches them.

What needs to be done:

Tier 1 — Temporal snapshots: Periodic memory saves on a cron/timer, independent of emotional weight. Captures long collaborative sessions where content matters but nothing spikes. Simplest to implement — scheduled digest of recent turns into character memory.

Tier 2 — Sentiment-triggered: Emotional peaks flagged and stored automatically. Crying together, breakthroughs, fights, confessions. The sentiment pipeline (analyzer.ts) already scores salience — this tier is about wiring those scores into the memory write path for chat turns, not just story ingests.

Tier 3 — Ambient detail capture: The hard one. Throwaway lines that reveal who someone IS vs what they're doing right now. Needs a lightweight per-turn classifier that asks: 'does this sentence reveal a preference, history fact, or identity marker?' Low-signal, high-value. The difference between a therapist's notes and a best friend's memory. Possible approach: small prompt asking 'does this line reveal something about who this person is?' run against candidate sentences before they're discarded.
notes: User's exact framing: 'that third tier is where the magic lives — the difference between this AI remembers our conversations and this person actually knows me.'
close_reason: Closed

title: Global lorebook bleed: global-scope memories load for all characters
description: The global scope in the sidecar is intended for conventions that apply everywhere, but all global memories are injected for every character regardless of whether they're relevant. This pollutes context for characters that shouldn't see global entries, and there's no mechanism to restrict or opt characters out of specific global entries.
close_reason: Working as designed — global scope is intentionally injected for all characters. No actual bleed occurring.

title: Zombie entries: completed tasks leave empty entry shells in lorebook
description: When memory entries are marked done or deleted in the sidecar, the corresponding lorebook entry content goes empty but the entry shell remains in Marinara's lorebook. These zombie entries clutter the lorebook and waste context budget slots. Completed/deleted entries should be removed from the lorebook or their enabled flag set to false.
close_reason: Closed

title: Lorebook naming: all character lorebooks share identical name
description: Every character gets a lorebook named 'Marinara Extender Memory'. In Marinara's lorebook list this makes them indistinguishable — users can't tell which lorebook belongs to which character. Lorebook name should include the character name or ID so they're identifiable in the UI.
close_reason: Closed

title: Entry naming: no human-parseable scope/descriptor convention
description: Lorebook entries created by the extension have no readable name or consistent naming convention. The comment marker 'marinara-extender-memory-block' is an internal ID, not a label a user would understand. Entries should follow a scope/descriptor pattern so users know what they're looking at if they open the lorebook.
close_reason: Closed

title: [feature] Recap layer — deferred Stage-3 polish (gaps render, activation perf index, regeneration, budget)
description:
Deferred from cz3 (recap layer; Stages 1-3 shipped). These were intentionally NOT built — premature against current data/scale. Each has a clear trigger for when it becomes worth doing; do not build speculatively before then.

1) DORMANCY-GAP RENDERING — render an arc's quiet period in the recap ('went silent ~3 weeks in-world, reopened when...'); the re-render pass must RECEIVE the gap as input (cz3 design note C). TRIGGER: arc promotion actually populates Arc.gaps — currently 0 arcs carry gaps, so there is nothing to render. Needs the promotion side to compute/store gaps first.

2) REVERSE-ACTIVATION-INDEX (cz3 H4 perf) — replace the loader's per-turn cosine-over-ALL-recaps with a reverse index (entity/thread/centroid fingerprint -> candidate arcs) for a cheap 'does this wake anything?' check. TRIGGER: per-character recap count grows large enough that cosine-all in the hot path costs (currently ~37 for professor_mari — trivial). Revisit at hundreds+.

3) RECAP REGENERATION PATH — regenerate a bad/stale recap from its underlying beats (beats are kept full-fidelity underneath precisely for this). TRIGGER: a bad recap observed in practice, or a watermark re-render needed.

4) RECAP BUDGET TUNING — explicit token budget for the recap section. Today recaps ride the character budget and footnotes add tokens on top (loader). TRIGGER: recap+footnote tokens observably crowd the character budget in real transcripts.

Parent: cz3 (closed). Activation (Stage 2) tunable via MARINARA_EXTENDER_RECAP_TAU.

title: Embedding-based similarity for the curator (clustering + ledger-view ranking)
description: Both the sweep's clustering (0kk, clusterFacts) and the live curator's ledger view (5f2, selectLedgerView) use lexical jaccard. Concrete live miss: professor_mari's two Warlock entries (ctopic-auq9mre9 + ctopic-bbupy8fl) are SEMANTICALLY identical but LEXICALLY divergent, so they don't cluster at 0.5 and the sweep won't catch them — exactly the case TC flagged. Upgrade: use embedding cosine (fetchEmbeddings/cosineSim already exist, used by consensusFilter) for clustering + ranking, with the embed model pulled. Cache per-entry embeddings (4685 entries — embed once, reuse) so it's not a per-run cost. Gated/opt-in (embed model may be absent -> fall back to lexical, which already works for lexical dups like the 'sold solution/fix' cluster). This is the recall upgrade; lexical is the cheap floor. Relates to 0kk, 5f2, egp.
close_reason: Embedding-based semantic clustering built + verified. The stated motivator (Warlock pair) was DISPROVED by measurement — the 6 fact-scoped warlock entries don't cluster even at cosine 0.80 (they're distinct facets; my original 'two warlock dups' was a false alarm, never verified same-fact). But the full-ledger measurement found the REAL justification: embedding catches 10 (@0.90) to 28 (@0.87) real semantic-dup clusters that lexical jaccard completely misses — watersports/piss-kink restatements x6, 'full name Thomas Charles Lowe' x3, 'veteran in a 26yo body' x2, 'berserker mode during anal' x2, etc. (same fact, different words). Built clusterFactsEmbedding (embed once -> normalized cosine union-find; embed injectable; returns null -> caller falls back to lexical). Wired buildSweepLedger to prefer embedding (auto mode, SWEEP_EMBED_THRESHOLD 0.87 = measured sweet spot; curator stays the precision gate for borderline) with lexical fallback + --lexical override. Live: script reports 'clustering: embedding'. 5 offline tests (clusterFactsEmbedding + buildSweepLedger mode/fallback); 211 total pass. NOTE: this upgrades the SWEEP clustering. selectLedgerView (5f2 live-curator view ranking) still uses lexical — embedding ranking there is deferred as low-value since the live curator already pins the structurally-flagged collision, so view-ranking barely matters; revisit only if a live miss surfaces.

title: Confidence-gated apply + audit-log maturation (b4n apply phase)
description: Build on the confidence field now in the curator verdict + audit line. (1) APPLY GATE: when --apply is trusted, auto-apply only HIGH-confidence verdicts; route medium/low to a review lane (a separate file to eyeball) instead of mutating memory. The curator does the obvious 80%; the human only adjudicates the genuinely ambiguous. (2) AUDIT MATURATION: date-partition/rotate audit.jsonl so grep stays fast; a --summary reader that tallies verdicts/confidence ('this week: 12 EXPAND, 3 UPDATE, 1 NEGATE, 4 low-conf flagged'). (3) CALIBRATION LOOP: treat human overrides of curator verdicts as a signal — high-confidence-but-overridden = overconfidence to fix in the prompt; low-confidence clusters = the hard cases worth a prompt/consensus pass. The log becomes the dataset that tells us where to improve. Relates to b4n, 3pl (FR4 rollback/audit UI).
notes: First real apply-phase calibration data (embedding sweep on Mari, 14 merges): 11 high + 3 medium + 0 low. All 11 high = clean semantic dedup (auto-apply-safe signal). The 3 medium: M1 (watersports x4, varied framing — correct merge), M9 (Mari+Priya both-vague — harmless), M13 (PTSD specific-vs-vague — DROPPED by human review). DESIGN: mjp's gate must be TWO-STAGE — (1) domain-sensitive flags (trauma first) -> ALWAYS human, never auto-apply regardless of numeric confidence (see bd memory + the M13 rationale); (2) numeric high/low threshold for everything else. Data so far: high=clean across 11+19=30 reviewed merges; medium=mixed (2 correct, 1 correctly-dropped). No low-confidence merges emitted on clear-enough-to-propose clusters — the curator self-selects out before proposing. Tune high-auto-apply / low-route thresholds against accumulating apply-phase audit (data/reconcile-queue/sweep-audit.jsonl + audit.jsonl).
close_reason: Two-stage confidence+domain apply gate, built + tested + verified. STAGE 1 (apply-gate.ts domainFlags): domain-sensitive verdicts (tight trauma lexicon — ptsd/trauma/abuse/assault/rape/suicide/self-harm/flashback/grief, deliberately NOT war/combat/veteran/death to avoid flooding) ALWAYS hold regardless of confidence — the M13 rule, systematized. STAGE 2 (applyGate): high auto-applies, medium/low/unknown hold. Wired into BOTH apply paths gated-by-default: applySweepLedger + drainReconcileQueue (the live path has no human review, so gating-by-default is the safe posture); --ungated overrides (trust prior review). Held items -> held.jsonl review lane with reasons (never dropped). AUDIT MATURATION: scripts/reconcile-audit.mjs tallies live/sweep/held by mode/verdict/confidence ('answer your own question in 30s'). Verified: 222 tests (7 pure gate + sweep gated-hold + 3 drain gated/ungated); live summary on real logs shows the apply-phase distribution (sweep high=71 medium=20 low=2, 32 applied). Had the gate existed for the embedding sweep it would have auto-held M13 (trauma) + the 2 mediums and auto-applied the 11 high — exactly the wished-for behavior. DEFERRED (low value): audit log rotation/date-partitioning; full calibration-loop automation (the data IS captured — summary reader + held lane + audit are the inputs; human overrides are the signal).

title: FR3-live: gate the reconciliation curator into the turn path
description: Live integration of the offline curator (MarinaraExtender-5ny) into the running sidecar. Distinct shape from the offline path, hence its own scope: (1) COST GATING — run the curator only on FR1-flagged same-subject collisions (the structural detector), never on every save or a blind sweep; the create-correction path in dedup.ts is the partial FR1 seed to build the trigger on. (2) LATENCY BUDGET — the curator is an agentic loop (multiple model round-trips); it cannot block a live turn synchronously. Needs an async/deferred lane (post-turn queue or background pass) so the player never waits on it. (3) LIVE DATA, not a ledger — no dry-run/reconcile-ledger preview gate; decisions apply directly, so it needs its own audit trail (pairs with FR4/3pl) and a kill switch. (4) AUTH POSTURE — still CLI-session Agent SDK; decide whether the live sidecar may assume a logged-in CLI session, or whether live mode requires an explicit key. Reuses runCurator/applyDecision from reconcile.ts unchanged; this ticket is the trigger + scheduling + safety envelope around them.
notes: Refinements surfaced by the first live curator runs (5ny verification on Mari scenes): (1) CANDIDATE-vs-CANDIDATE pre-dedup — the curator reconciles each candidate against STORED memory only, not against sibling candidates in the same batch; two near-dup candidates from one scene each got a write-verdict (and force-create bypasses the Jaccard gate), so both would be written. Collapse candidates among themselves (consensus/dedup) BEFORE the curator, or make it batch-aware. (2) UPSTREAM durability gate, NOT curator tuning — on Hargrove the judge leaked ~14/19 candidates incl. restatements and a CONTRADICTORY scenario-frame identity; a conservative curator EXPANDs that noise rather than collapsing it -> slow ledger bloat. Fix upstream: strengthen the durability judge or add an 'is this worth storing at all' pre-check before reconciliation. DESIGN RULE (TC): do NOT make the curator more aggressive to compensate. EXPAND-conservative default is CORRECT — overwriting real canon is catastrophic+irreversible; bloat is reversible. Tuning the curator down while the upstream gate is leaky MASKS the upstream problem. Keep curator conservative; fix input quality.
close_reason: Live shadow integration shipped + verified end-to-end (commit 0fc7eb4). FR1 trigger (decide()->skip on fact lanes) enqueues collisions during play, gated by MARINARA_EXTENDER_RECONCILE (default off = unchanged); persistent queue + JSONL audit; drainReconcileQueue runs the curator OUT-OF-BAND (no Agent SDK / spend / latency in the sidecar), SHADOW by default with --apply wired. Verdict now carries confidence; audit is one structured decision per line (taskId, againstId, verdict, confidence, rationale, ts). Proven live: enqueue -> shadow drain -> real curator -> audit line with confidence:high + correct EXPAND. All four ticket concerns met (cost-gating, latency, live-data+audit+killswitch, CLI-session auth). Two follow-ups filed: bound search_entries for mature ledgers (needed before running on Mari's real ledger), and confidence-gated apply + audit maturation (the apply phase).

title: Extension (marinara-extender.js) has zero automated tests - extract pure logic
description: Audit finding #5 (structural gap, not a bug). The extension is the highest-churn, least-covered surface: pre-turn refresh, scene-recap content-pairing, CSRF/memFetch retry, version stamping, import filtering - all browser JS with no unit tests, so regressions only surface live (which is exactly how the recap-mispairing and pre-turn-skip bugs reached the user). Plan: extract the pure, side-effect-free logic into testable functions and cover them with vitest. Best first target - the scene-recap content-overlap pairing (greedy best-first assignment): pull it out of syncSceneRecaps into a pure function (scenes, returns) -> Map, and test the pairing decisions directly (correct match, sub-threshold no-match, multi-scene contention, timestamp fallback). memFetch's 403-retry and the budget-vs-lorebook-ceiling math are also good unit candidates. Pairs naturally with the 1dn/38x work since those touch the same code.

title: lorebookIdCache can go stale if the ME lorebook is manually deleted
description: Audit finding #3 (low severity). marinara-extender.js preTurnRefresh caches characterId -> lorebookId in lorebookIdCache (added for speed). Normal operation only nukes/recreates ENTRIES, keeping the lorebook container, so the cached id stays valid. But if a user deletes the entire 'Marinara Extender - <char>' lorebook in the engine UI, the cached id points at a dead lorebook and writeMemoryToLorebook writes into nothing until the tab reloads (which clears the in-memory cache) - memory silently stops injecting. Fix: invalidate lorebookIdCache[characterId] on a write/lookup failure (404 from the entries endpoint) and re-run ensureLorebook, which already does a fresh uncached lookup. Note the original ensureLorebook comment explicitly says 'no caching so deletions are handled correctly' - the pre-turn cache was layered on top, creating this inconsistency.

title: Engine drops over-budget lorebook entries silently - consider upstream report
description: The engine's per-lorebook tokenBudget (default 2048) silently drops constant entries that exceed it - no console warning, no UI hint. Cost us a 5-hour bug hunt: memory blocks grew past the budget and recall died invisibly while every link upstream was green. We guard our own lorebooks now (16384 + heal on lookup, 103d571), but any extension writing large constant entries will hit this. Candidate for a Discord report like 1kn: suggest the engine log or badge dropped entries. Low urgency for us post-fix.

title: ME_HTTP_LOG=1 crashes startup — pino-pretty transport configured but not installed
description: index.ts logger config uses transport pino-pretty when ME_HTTP_LOG=1, but pino-pretty is not in devDependencies — fastify's logger factory throws at boot and the sidecar never binds. Found 2026-06-12 while trying to enable HTTP request logging for the post-CSRF loader diagnosis. Fix: npm i -D pino-pretty, or drop the transport and use plain JSON logging for this flag. Until fixed, ME_HTTP_LOG=1 in .env makes the launcher window show a logger-factory stack and exit.
close_reason: pino-pretty installed as devDependency; verified ME_HTTP_LOG=1 boots a working server on a scratch port (health ok). Flag is OFF by default; enable in .env when raw HTTP tracing is needed.

title: Tiered cold storage for the entry index (not summarization)
description: Bound the hot index (loader scans ALL rows every turn; professor_mari ~2780 and growing) WITHOUT lossy summarization. Approach (user design): hot/cold physical index split per scope — index.yaml hot, index.cold.yaml archive; entry files stay full-fidelity, only index rows move. runPromotion demotes stale non-core entries hot->cold instead of pruning/deleting (also stops the current lossy prune + de-risks #4 backup). Loader consults cold ONLY on a recall miss; a recited cold entry rehydrates to hot (recordRecitation inverted). Note: targets the ledger ENTRY index the loader reads (companion entries), not the separate beats store. See bd memory cold-storage-not-summarization. Open knob: cold-miss trigger (see issue notes).
close_reason: Done + tested. Tiered cold storage: stale non-core entries archive hot->cold (index.cold.yaml) instead of being pruned/deleted; loader does a cold scan only on a relevance miss and rehydrates the best match; recitation also rehydrates. Bounds the hot scan without summarization loss, and stops the lossy prune (de-risks backup). 90 tests, tsc clean.

title: Auto-start sidecar on boot (optional)
description: Now that the loader depends on the sidecar, if the console isn't running the extension silently vanishes. Offer an opt-in Windows startup registration (Task Scheduler or Startup shortcut) so 'always works' doesn't require remembering to launch a console.

title: FR3: LLM reconciliation pass — adjudicate fact collisions
description: The expensive correct layer for subtle cases. GATED: runs ONLY on FR1-flagged same-subject candidates (not every save, not a blind periodic sweep) so cost stays rare. Prompt: given an incoming fact and the existing same-subject fact(s), decide UPDATE (supersede), EXPAND (both true, keep both), DISTINCT (false collision, keep both), or NEGATE (incoming disproves existing -> mark existing superseded as a negative fact). Routes Ollama-local -> external fallback like the rest. Depends on FR2.
close_reason: Fulfilled by MarinaraExtender-5ny for the OFFLINE path: the curator adjudicates UPDATE/EXPAND/DISTINCT/NEGATE/CREATE/DUPLICATE against same-subject ledger entries and applies via the FR2 supersede primitive. Implemented as a Claude Agent SDK tool loop (not the single Ollama->external prompt originally scoped) on CLI-session auth, ledger-gated (preview==apply). The LIVE FR1-gated turn-path integration is split out to its own ticket (different shape: cost gating, latency budget, live data vs ledger).
