title: Decide: should mere exposure (retrievalCount on load) still drive promotion?
description: computeScore = retrievalCount + recitationCount*3. retrievalCount still increments on every LOAD, so an entry that merely surfaces a lot can promote to core without ever being demonstrably used. lastRetrievedAt is now use-only (honest), but score still rewards exposure. Decision needed: de-weight or drop retrievalCount from computeScore (promotion = proven use), weighed against recitation detection's blind spot (it misses paraphrased use, so pure-recitation promotion could under-credit genuinely-useful-but-not-echoed entries). Deferred for a deliberate call.
close_reason: Resolved: exposure credit is now relevance-gated in loader.ts selectEntries — retrievalCount increments only when an entry is SUMMONED (relevanceScore > 0.1), not when it merely rides in on the recency fallback. So mere exposure no longer drives promotion; relevance-driven exposure does, which becomes a more honest proxy for use as the Current cache improves. computeScore (retrievalCount + recitationCount*3) kept as-is. See commit.

title: User-requested memory save: persist facts/preferences on command
description: Users should be able to ask the AI to 'remember this' mid-conversation and have the extender write it to the appropriate lorebook tier. Examples: 'remember I prefer dark themes', 'save this list', 'remember my character's backstory'. The AI would emit a structured tag or signal, the extension would intercept it, classify the scope (chat vs character vs global), and call POST /api/entries to persist it as a named entry. Scope selection could be heuristic or user-directed via phrasing.
notes: Implemented via prompt directive (user-chosen approach over extension interception). The [remember:] command pipeline already handled extraction, dedup, scope routing, and tiering end-to-end; the only gap was that explicit user requests weren't a guaranteed trigger — the instructions discouraged trivial saves, so 'remember I prefer dark themes' would be dropped as minor. Added a 'WHEN THE USER ASKS YOU TO REMEMBER' block to MEMORY_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS making a direct request a HARD trigger that overrides the discretionary rule, with lane/scope guidance (fact→user_topics, task→open_threads; character scope unless user says this-chat-only), a confirmation nudge, and a guard distinguishing real requests from incidental phrasing ('remember when we…'). Verified extractRememberTags parses the emitted bracket format incl. spaced content values. PENDING: live browser verification after sidecar restart.
close_reason: Implemented as prompt directive over the existing [remember:] pipeline; type-checks clean, parser path verified. Live browser test pending sidecar restart.

title: [task] Measure pre-turn budget headroom — how much of PRE_TURN_BUDGET_MS we actually use
description:
We block generation up to PRE_TURN_BUDGET_MS (800ms, marinara-extender.js) for preTurnRefresh, but we have no measurement of how much of that lead time we actually consume — so we can't tell if we're comfortable or at the edge. This matters more now: 38x (dual-retrieval) turned pre-turn from ONE sidecar call into N (one per participant, each including the Stage-2 recap embedding) + N lorebook writes. We told the user 'always-on, add a toggle if the bite is too big' — this is the instrument that tells us whether/when that's needed, instead of guessing.

WHAT: instrument preTurnRefresh to report lead-time usage:
- total elapsed from start to when the awaited refreshes settle (or the budget timeout fires);
- whether the budget timeout fired and how many participants were still pending (stragglers that lagged to next turn);
- ideally per-participant timing (which character's refresh is slow — pre-turn call vs lorebook write) and participant count.
Console log at minimum (e.g. '[ME] pre-turn: 540/800ms, 3/3 participants, 0 stragglers'); optionally surface a rolling stat in the panel Settings or /api/health so it's visible without console-diving.

WHY P4: no pain observed yet; this is pre-emptive observability. ESCALATE earlier if multi-character scenes feel laggy, prompts balloon, or we're deciding on the dual-retrieval toggle (38x) and want data first. Relates to 38x.

title: serializedWrite keyed by raw path string - case-insensitive FS edge
description: Audit finding #4 (theoretical/very low severity). storage.ts serializedWrite serializes per filePath using a Map keyed by the raw string. On case-insensitive filesystems (Windows, default macOS), two different-cased path strings for the same physical file ('Foo.yaml' vs 'foo.yaml') would hash to different keys and NOT serialize, allowing a concurrent read-modify-write race that could corrupt YAML. Mitigated in practice because all paths come from the same path-building helpers (entryPath/indexPath/etc.) which emit consistent strings, so divergent casing should never occur. Fix if we ever want belt-and-suspenders: normalize the lock key (e.g. path.resolve + toLowerCase on win32) before Map lookup. Filing for awareness; not currently reachable.

title: v1.1: IDF-weighted relevance scoring (retire stopword list)
description:
Post-MVP tweak — defer until the holding-pool epic and core features are working. Replace the equal-weight lexical containment in loader.ts relevanceScore with IDF-weighted containment so distinctive tokens (Emma, cover-letter, Hargrove) drive relevance instead of being drowned out by formulaic beat-summary vocabulary (feel/trust/wants/anxious). Subsumes and retires the hand-maintained RELEVANCE_STOPWORDS list.

DESIGN NOTES (from review):
- It's IDF, not TF-IDF: summaries are ~15 words so TF~=1 and does nothing. Score = sum(idf of matched summary tokens) / sum(idf of all summary tokens), preserving the 0-1 range and the directional summary->recentText property.
- Corpus = PER CHARACTER, not per-scope. A fresh chat has too few entries for stable IDF; token rarity is a property of the character's domain. Build one idf table per character, reuse across that character's chat/character/global-relevant scopes.
- Smoothing: df+1 for unseen tokens so a brand-new distinctive token gets ~max weight (correct) rather than divide-by-zero.
- Build cost is trivial (one pass, ~18k tokens for a 900-entry character). Rebuild lazily on the promotion pass or every N saves; cache in memory keyed by character. Per-turn lookup stays O(summary tokens) — zero added latency, no model call.
- RECALIBRATE the RELEVANCE_CREDIT_THRESHOLD (currently 0.1 in loader.ts): IDF weighting shifts the score distribution, so the 'summoned' gate that drives retrievalCount/exposure-credit changes meaning. Re-tune against real data, don't assume 0.1 holds.
- Alternative considered: embeddings cosine (reuses Ollama EMBED_MODEL) — rejected for v1.1 because it trades the zero-latency/zero-cost property for a per-turn model call. IDF keeps the cheap-lookup property.

title: Beat index writes unserialized; config path breaks on spaces
description: upsertBeatIndex does unlocked read-modify-write (race class). config.ts CONFIG_DIR uses import.meta.url pathname which percent-encodes spaces; use fileURLToPath.
close_reason: upsertBeatIndex serialized per character; config.ts uses fileURLToPath (handles spaces/drive letters).

value: Workflow pattern (TC, validated on the ArcStatus/EntryStatus audit): when ADDING a value to a TypeScript union type that crosses a SERIALIZATION boundary (persisted to YAML/JSON, sent/accepted over an API, or produced/validated from LLM output), the TS compiler will NOT find all the consumers you must update — there is no exhaustiveness guarantee unless someone wrote assertNever, and storage layers often do 'parse(raw) as T' with NO runtime validation, so unknown values round-trip silently. Do a TWO-PASS grep: (1) grep the TYPE NAME (e.g. EntryStatus) to find typed consumers and switches; (2) grep the existing VALUES as STRING LITERALS (e.g. 'open' | 'in_progress' | 'done' | 'deferred') to find the allow-lists, default filters, and coercions that operate on plain strings and never mention the type. The SILENT failures live in pass 2 — concrete examples from this codebase: VALID_STATUSES at api.ts:102 rejects unknowns; digest.ts:207 coerces unknowns to 'open'; api.ts:137 default filter excludes them; loader.ts:332 leaks them into the prompt. None of these are reachable by reasoning — all empirical. Corollary design move: when a new value is really a different concern (e.g. lifecycle 'dormant' vs entry status), keep it on a SEPARATE type and let the two type systems meet at exactly one deliberate consumer, rather than widening the shared serialized enum.

Project ergonomic posture (TC's call, defended): ship capability features DEFAULT-ON with an env kill switch (opt-OUT), NOT default-off with a 'did you know you can enable it' footnote. Reason: opt-in causes SILENT DEGRADATION — the median user gets restricted results that look fine because they have nothing to compare to, and support cannot diagnose 'feature does not catch X' because half the user base runs a different feature set than the docs describe. Cost framing used to justify: a 274MB ollama model pull is the same mental category as the step-1 model download the user already accepted, and calling a different endpoint on an already-running ollama is not a new service. First applied to the kNN embedding candidate-generator (MarinaraExtender-cz3 / knn-packaging child); mirrors the time-sense feature.

Marinara Engine runs client extensions under a strict CSP: script-src 'self' blob: https://sdk.scdn.co — NO 'unsafe-eval'. So eval() and new Function() are BLOCKED (confirmed live: 'EvalError: call to Function() blocked by CSP'). The extensions reference doc's claim that extensions execute via new Function('marinara', ext.js) is misleading for execution purposes — Marinara actually loads extension code via a blob: URL <script> (CSP allows blob:). Implication for our loader stub: it must NOT use new Function/eval; to run fetched code it wraps it as (function(marinara){...})(globalBridge) and injects a Blob <script>, setting window.__marinaraExtender = marinara first to bridge the scoped API. Marinara serves at http://127.0.0.1:7860 over plain HTTP (no mixed-content issue fetching the 3001 sidecar).

Marinara Engine scene lifecycle (verified vs Pasta-Devs/Marinara-Engine main: routes/scene.routes.ts + services/storage/chats.storage.ts). A SCENE is a chat branch — there is NO scene.storage; scenes persist via chats.storage. POST /api/scene/conclude generates a ~200-word VIVID NARRATIVE PROSE summary of the concluded scene via an LLM call ('this summary will become a permanent memory for the character(s)'), then PERSISTS it three sidecar-readable ways: (1) injects it as a message in the ORIGIN conversation (persona and char returned from their scene + summary text), (2) appends to each involved character's memories array as the string '[Scene on DATE] {summary}', (3) sets scene chat metadata sceneStatus=concluded. Returns SceneConcludeResponse with a summary field. Separately, chat rows carry their own summary column (default null; set by the chat-summary POST agent for long chats). IMPLICATION for the recap-layer (see recap-layer-v1.1): scene-arc recaps are FREE — the engine already emits a dated, named, persisted proto-recap per scene = the chronological FLOOR. It is PROSE (not the structured threads/open-questions shape) and never reaches ACROSS scenes. So the sidecar's only expensive job is the cross-scene THROUGH-LINE arc recap (thematic ceiling) the engine structurally cannot make. sceneStatus=concluded is a free deterministic promotion trigger for the floor; the branches x beats_per_session > visibility_window density formula applies ONLY to the through-line tier.

Reconciliation curator (5ny/FR3) uses the Claude Agent SDK (@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk) on CLI-SESSION auth by default — no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, no baseURL. SDK credential resolution: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -> logged-in claude CLI session -> claude.ai. This is the ONLY Anthropic-coupled path in the extender; everything else stays OpenAI-compatible (Ollama/linkapi). The SDK speaks native Anthropic wire format, NOT OpenAI-compat, so it cannot ride the linkapi upstream.

Engine (Pasta-Devs/Marinara-Engine) has a RECURRING CLASS of file-durability / crash-safety bugs: fsync no-ops, .bak not durably flushed (closed #631; the flushFile 'r' read-only-fd Windows no-op = 1kn; 2+ more of the same class on Discord). Do NOT assume engine file writes are crash-safe. The EXTENDER's own atomicWriteFile (memory-extender/src/storage.ts:183) is the hardened reference implementation — fsyncs the WRITABLE handle (not a read-only 'r' fd), warns-once on fsync failure instead of swallowing, atomic rename with Windows EPERM/EBUSY/EACCES transient-retry. Extender storage is independently durable; these are upstream's bugs, not ours.

Overarching memory design law: FEELINGS ACCUMULATE, FACTS SUPERSEDE. Emotional beats (character_topics) accumulate over time — the arc is the point. Facts (user_topics) supersede: a later fact replaces an earlier one, but the old one is NOT deleted — it is marked superseded with a pointer to its replacement (tier move, not delete), so a disproven/negative fact stays queryable (you said Mei before — did you mean Lin?). Maps onto the existing Current(active) vs retention(kept) axis: superseded = kept-but-not-current. open_threads already have an open/in_progress/done lifecycle. Key trap: 0.35 Jaccard dedup EATS corrections because sister-is-Mei vs sister-is-Lin differ only in the meaning-carrying token (same blind spot as equal-weight relevance — see the IDF issue). Fix order matters: lane-aware dedup (stop dropping) then lane-aware supersession then LLM reconciliation (GATED by the structural collision detector, not a blind sweep) then rollback/audit UI.

Ledger Pattern corollary - ASSEMBLY DISCIPLINE: when you run N passes to beat single-pass variance, naive UNION (OR every pass) is the anti-pattern at the assemble step - it keeps near-dupes, overwhelms the downstream filter, and preserves a 1-of-N mis-attribution as if it were signal (found live: 3-pass fact extraction -> 74 candidates, judge kept 71, a 1/3 Priya-vs-Mari mis-attribution survived). Correct aggregation = CONSENSUS in a NORMALIZED space: normalize each item to a canonical identity (embedding-similarity cluster or cheap LLM normalize) BEFORE counting, then keep iff it recurs in floor(N/2)+1 passes with consistent attribution. More passes must mean higher confidence, not more noise. The multi-pass COUNT and the multi-pass AGGREGATION are two separate disciplines. See docs/ledger-pattern.md.

v1.1 architecture commitment: the RECAP LAYER is THE headline feature. Cold storage was symptom treatment ('too many entries'); the disease is beat fragmentation that loses NARRATIVE COHERENCE at scale — a loader pulling 3 high-signal beats without connective tissue yields disconnected moments that don't add up to a story (knowing facts vs knowing a story). A recap entry reconstructs the story: it organizes N underlying beats by ARC and becomes the canonical retrieval unit the loader injects instead of fragments. Aggregation unit is the ARC (a named through-line, e.g. 'Priya-as-co-experimenter: drafted methodology -> wanted n=10 protocols -> negotiating her role in Mari experiments'), NOT a topical cluster — a character runs multiple simultaneous arcs, and arc identity is INFERRED (LLM judgment).

TWO-TIER DIVISION OF LABOR (verified against the engine, see marinara-scene-conclude-persists-summary): (1) SCENE-ARC RECAPS = the chronological FLOOR, ingested FREE — the engine already emits a dated, named, persisted ~200-word prose proto-recap at /api/scene/conclude, and sceneStatus=concluded is a free deterministic promotion trigger. The sidecar just ingests these as recap leaves with beats as footnotes; no LLM cost. (2) THROUGH-LINE ARC RECAPS = the thematic CEILING, the cross-scene narrative the engine STRUCTURALLY CANNOT make (it summarizes within one scene boundary) = the sidecar's defensible value-add. This is the ONLY expensive call.

Mechanics: ASSIGN-ON-PROMOTION — arc identity inferred with hindsight by an LLM pass in runPromotion (NOT the hot path), gated to promotion-eligible (already-proven-important) beats only, so the big call is never wasted. Lane-aware: feeling-recap = trajectory/path (preserve the arc), fact-recap = current point + superseded footnotes. Drift handled by WATERMARK (recap covers a closed prefix 'current through beat N'; newer beats ride hot until next promotion advances N; resolved threads = watermark pinned at end). Beats stay full-fidelity underneath as cited footnotes = regeneration path for bad recaps. The branches x beats_per_session > visibility_window density formula applies ONLY to the through-line tier. Evolves cold-storage-not-summarization (the earlier plumbing fix for the same disease). OPEN: how the promotion pass clusters beats across scenes into one through-line is the next design spike.

The Ledger Pattern (TC, design pattern): to process a large text input through a context-limited LLM, window it, process one slice at a time against a durable per-input ledger (temp file - resumable + inspectable), and assemble at the end. Prompt-integrity rule: partition the context ~1/6 prompt, ~1/6 input chunk, ~2/3 headroom - keep the input slice <= ~1/6 of the window so the model reasons instead of triaging. Window scales with the model's context (a fixed chunk count is the anti-pattern). A big single prompt makes a model triage and drop quiet results (the Warlock fact vanished in a 10-chunk window, recovered in a focused one). Full writeup: memory-extender/docs/ledger-pattern.md. Already partly embodied by the import pipeline (incremental/resumable beats) and the backfill/repair scripts.

Verified in production 2026-06-11 (post crash-recovery, rebuilt-from-empty entries table): extension lorebook holds exactly 2 Memory System entries per character, stable. The 30-duplicate/zero-length entries seen in the 2026-05-17 profile export were pre-fix wreckage from MarinaraExtender-f6x (tiers bypassing dedup) and MarinaraExtender-634 (zombie empty shells), both closed; _lorebookWriteChain mutex (dee592c, 6/1) holds. Curator design principle (TC, validated on Mari/Hargrove live run): keep the reconciliation curator EXPAND-conservative by default — never tune it more aggressive to fight ledger bloat. Cost of overwriting/nuking real canon is catastrophic + irreversible; cost of bloat is reversible. The curator correctly chose DISTINCT (not NEGATE) on a contradictory scenario-frame artifact, refusing to overwrite real identity — that asymmetry is the point. Ledger bloat from marginal EXPANDs is an UPSTREAM problem (leaky durability judge); fix it there. Tuning the curator to compensate masks the upstream leak. See [[curator-applydecision-must-force-create-savefact-opts-force]] and b4n.

Marinara Engine GET /api/chats/:id/messages returns ALL messages by default (storage.listMessages = SELECT ... WHERE chatId ORDER BY createdAt,id with no LIMIT). Pagination is opt-in via ?limit=N&before=cursor (listMessagesPaginated). Our extension fetchChatMessages sends no limit, so it gets the full history; the only client cap is .slice(-5000). Confirmed against Pasta-Devs/Marinara-Engine main, routes/chats.routes.ts + services/storage/chats.storage.ts.

mjp REQUIREMENT — domain-sensitive confidence flags (a layer ABOVE the numeric thresholds). Medium-confidence curator verdicts on TRAUMA-ADJACENT facts must ALWAYS route to human review, never auto-apply, regardless of where the high/low thresholds sit. Trauma is the FIRST domain-sensitive category; the gate is two-stage: (1) domain flags -> always human; (2) numeric confidence for the rest. Rationale (TC, the M13 case): a specific-vs-vague merge on a trauma memory is ASYMMETRIC — wrong-merge permanently erases a possible second trauma memory the system was trying to surface (unrecoverable lead loss); right-merge saves only a near-dup row, and the vague sibling adds nothing the dense canonical (Samara/RPG/Wood's death/op-tempo) already carries. Generalizes: when right-merge upside ~= 0 and wrong-merge downside is unrecoverable, never auto-apply regardless of confidence. See [[curator-design-principle-tc-validated-on-mari-hargrove]].

Design law for index growth (#5 compaction): TIERED COLD STORAGE, not summarization. Memories are never compressed/summarized (lossy) — stale ones DEMOTE out of the hot index into a cold archive at full fidelity, consulted only on a recall MISS. Maps onto human memory (old = harder to reach, not gone; retrieval strengthens). Implementation on existing code: (1) physical split per scope — index.yaml (hot, scanned every turn) + index.cold.yaml (archive, read only on miss); entry files stay put, only the index ROW moves. (2) runPromotion already computes daysSinceRetrieval — add a stale-non-core -> cold transition that REPLACES the lossy prune/delete path (so this also stops destroying old memories and de-risks backup). core/secondary_core never leave hot. (3) recordRecitation inverted: a recited COLD entry rehydrates back to hot. Cold scan only on miss, so per-turn cost stays bounded. Aligns with fact-vs-feeling-memory-principle (nothing deleted).

Curator applyDecision MUST force-create (saveFact opts.force -> dedup.createEntry, bypassing createEntryIfUnique's Jaccard gate). Non-obvious: for UPDATE/EXPAND/DISTINCT the candidate is lexically NEAR the fact it collided with, so the blind structural gate would re-collapse it into that fact and silently skip the write + supersede. The curator IS the dedup decision; the structural gate is redundant/harmful once it has ruled.
